SECTION "0: Vendor-specific Commands" 0x3F
{
    COMMAND "Customer_Extension" 0x000
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2048"=1
        doc "Customer-specific generic extensions to HCI commands."
        PARAM "Channel ID"
            uint8
            doc "The Channel ID is used to route the commands to the desired destiation."
            binary_message_only
            encode_value =  if      ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x03) then  0xF0
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x07) then  0xF0
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x08) then  0xF0
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x09) then  0xF0
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x90) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x21) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x05) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x85) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x86) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x89) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x01) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x12) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x25) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x27) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x31) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x39) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x40) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x88) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x91) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x93) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x95) then  0xF3
                            else if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x96) then  0xF3
                            else    0xFF; # Error

        PARAM "HCI_ext_opcode"
            uint8
            doc "This is the real extension opcode for each command."
            enum
            {
                {0x03, "PWR_CONSUMPTION"},
                {0x07, "ENCRYPTION_KEY_LENGTH_READ_CMD"},
                {0x08, "READ_ACL_RETRANSMISSION_COUNTER_CMD"},
                {0x09, "WRITE_COEX_ACL_PRIORITY"},
                {0x21, "LOC_INFO_READ"},
                {0x05, "LOC_PROD_INFO_READ"},
                {0x85, "LOC_SET_HOST_WAKE_UP"},
                {0x86, "LOC_READ_BT_WAKE_UP"},
                                {0x89, "LOC_WLAN_COEX_TEST"},
                {0x01, "LOC_SELF_TEST_RUN"},
                {0x12, "LOC_SELF_TEST_RESULT_GET"},
                {0x25, "LOC_BER_TEST_START"},
                {0x27, "LOC_BER_TEST_RESULT_GET"},
                {0x31, "LOC_BER_PER_TEST_RESULT_GET"},
                {0x39, "LOC_EVENT_FTD_ACTIVATE"},
                {0x40, "LOC_EVENT_FTD_DEACTIVATE"},
                {0x88, "LOC_AUDIO_ROUTING_CONTROL"},
                                {0x90, "LOC_SET_MAX_BT_POWER"},
                {0x91, "LOC_VENDOR_DEBUG_CONTROL"},
                {0x92, "LOC_DEBUG_FRAMEWORK_V1"},
                {0x93, "LOC_RSSI_MONITORING"},
                {0x95, "LOC_LE_ADV_REPORTING_CFG"},
                {0x96, "LOC_LE_RP_ADDR_RESOLUTION_CFG"}
            }
            default = 0x1;

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x90, "LOC_SET_MAX_BT_POWER"
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        PARAM "Set Max BT Power"
            int8
            doc     "Max BT Power"
            default = 0x0
            present_if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x90);
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x91, "LOC_VENDOR_DEBUG_CONTROL"     (Debug Framework)
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        PARAM "VendorDebugControl"
            uint8
            doc     "To enable/disable Vendor Debug Control (Debug Framework)"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Enable, Set DBFW's Trace Mode = PUSH"},
                {0x01, "Disable, Set DBFW's Trace Mode = STOP"}
            }
            present_if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x91);
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x92, "LOC_DEBUG_FRAMEWORK_V1"       (Debug Framework)
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        PARAM "DBFW_SubCmd"
            uint8
            doc "Sub Command"
            enum
            {
                {0x01, "Write Config"},
                {0x02, "Read Config and Status"},
                {0x03, "Poll Traces"},
                {0x04, "Poll Stack Dump from NV Log"},
                {0x0B, "Read dbuint32"},
                {0x0C, "Write dbuint32"}
            }
            present_if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92);
        PARAM "ModuleMask"
            uint32
            doc "Module Mask"
            default = 0x7FFFFFFF
            bitmap
            {
                {0x00000001, "DBFW"},
                {0x00000002, "BTC"},
                {0x00000004, "LC"},
                {0x00000008, "LM"},
                {0x00000010, "HCI"},
                {0x00000020, "TRAN"},
                {0x00000040, "FM"},
                                {0x00000080, "BCS"},
                                {0x00000100, "ULP"},
                                {0x00000200, "UHE"},
                {0x40000000, "Others"}
            }
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "Level"
            uint8
            doc "Trace Level"
            enum
            {
                {0x00,"DEBUG"},
                {0x01,"INFO"},
                {0x02,"WARNING"},
                {0x03,"CRITICAL"}
            }
            default = 0x01
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "TraceMode"
            uint8
            doc "Trace Mode"
            enum
            {
                {0x00,"STOP"},
                {0x01,"POLL"},
                {0x02,"PUSH"}
            }
            default = 0x02
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "Flow"
            uint8
            doc "Trace Flow"
            enum
            {
                {0x00,"STOP_WHEN_FULL"},
                {0x01,"OVERWRITE_WHEN_FULL"}
            }
            default = 0x01
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "PushTimeout_in_ms"
            uint16
            doc "in ms"
            default = 100
            min = 100
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "PushWatermark"
            uint16
            doc "Push Watermark"
            default = 8
            min = 4
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "Trace Dump Output Channel"
            uint8
            doc "Trace Dump Channel Selection"
            enum
            {
                {0x00,"HCI"},
                {0x01,"Debug UART Binary"},
                {0x02,"Debug UART ASCII"}
            }
            default = 0x00
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "Stack Dump Output Channel"
            uint8
            doc "Stack Dump Output Channel Selection"
            enum
            {
                {0x00,"HCI"},
                {0x01,"Debug UART Binary"},
                {0x02,"Debug UART ASCII"}
            }
            default = 0x00
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "Core Dump Output Channel"
            uint8
            doc "Core Dump Channel Selection"
            enum
            {
                {0x00,"HCI"},
                {0x01,"Debug UART Binary"},
                {0x02,"Debug UART ASCII"}
            }
            default = 0x00
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x01));
        PARAM "AssertIndex"
            uint8
            doc "Assert Index for Poll Stack Dump"
            min = 0x71
            max = 0x77
            default = 0x71
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x04));
        PARAM "index"
            uint32
            doc "index"
            default = 0
            min = 0
            max = 31
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x0C));
        PARAM "value0x92"
            uint32
            doc "index"
            default = 0
            present_if(("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x92) and ("DBFW_SubCmd" = 0x0C));
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x03, "PWR_CONSUMPTION"
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        # No parameters...

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x7, "ENCRYPTION_KEY_LENGTH_READ_CMD"
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    #       doc "This command reads the encryption key length that has been negotiated between"
    #           "remote and local Link Manager for the given connection handle."
    #           "The connection handle is only valid for point to point ACL connection handles"
    #           "(not valid for SCO or Broadcast handles)."
    #           "Connection handle: LSB first, value in 0x0000-0x0FFF"
    #   PARAM "Connection handle"
    #           (borrowed from 0x9, "WRITE_COEX_ACL_PRIORITY")

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x8, "READ_ACL_RETRANSMISSION_COUNTER_CMD"
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    #   PARAM "Connection handle"
    #           (borrowed from 0x9, "WRITE_COEX_ACL_PRIORITY")

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x9, "WRITE_COEX_ACL_PRIORITY"
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        PARAM "Connection handle"
            connhandle16
            doc     "Write the COEX Priority of a specified Connection Handle"
                "Connection handle value in 0x0000-0x0EFF (0xF00 - 0x0FFF Reserved for"
                " future use)."
            present_if (("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x7) or ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x08) or ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x09) or ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x93));

        PARAM "ACL Priority"
            uint8
            doc     "Defines the COEX Priorty of the ACL Link, can be High or Low Priority"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "LOW PRIORITY"},
                {0x01, "HIGH PRIORITY"}
            }
            present_if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x09);

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x21, "LOC_INFO_READ"
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        PARAM "Info-Type"
            uint8
            doc "Software version (LPRF_INFO_MCU_EXT_SW_VER):<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;Requests the software version of the Bluetooth SW<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;Reading LOCAL SW version: 020021 which is sent as 020021 encoded in an ASCII string.<br>"
                "Software checksum (LPRF_INFO_EXT_SW_CHECKSUM):<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;Requests the software checksum of the Bluetooth SW (firmware of the device/ROM).<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;It will be calculated and delivered as uint16 value (unsigned interger 16 bits).<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;The host will do the checksum matching. (LSB first)<br>"
                "Software Relase date (LPRF_SW_RELEASE_DAY_MONTH_YEAR):<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;Requests the month and uear of software rlease (build date). THe length of this<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;product information is four bytes (BCD).<br>"
                "Note: For example 30 November 2001 is 0x30 0x11 0x20 0x01 as a contiguous<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;sequence having no spaces.<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;The release should also be the numeric value assigned to that SW version as well<br>"
                "nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;nbsp;as the release date."
            enum
            {
                {0x01, "LPRF_INFO_MCU_EXT_SW_VER"},
                {0x02, "LPRF_INFO_EXT_SW_CHECKSUM"},
                {0x41, "LPRF_SW_RELEASE_DAY_MONTH_YEAR"}
            }
            present_if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x21);

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x05, "LOC_PROD_INFO_READ"
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        PARAM "Production Info-Type"
            uint8
            doc "Production Information:"
                "- Product HW Version (LPRF_PROD_HW):"
                "nbsp;nbsp;Requests the HW version of the Bluetooth device. The length of the HW"
                "nbsp;nbsp;Version is four bytes (ASCII character sequence)."
                "- Product Code (LPRF_PROD_CODE):"
                "nbsp;nbsp;Requests the production code. It identifies the product uniquely. It's a"
                "nbsp;nbsp;description of the chip component like with/without RAM, Flash, also for"
                "nbsp;nbsp;example it could be similar to the print on the cabinet. The length of the"
                "nbsp;nbsp;Product Code is seven bytes (ASCII character sequence)."
                "- Product Code Basic (LPRF_PROD_CODE_BASIC):"
                "nbsp;nbsp;Requests the product information described the kernel or core whereon the"
                "nbsp;nbsp;chip is based on. The length of this product information is seven bytes"
                "nbsp;nbsp;(ASCII character sequence)."
                "- Hardware Release Date (LPRF_HW_RELEASE_DAY_MONTH_YEAR):"
                "nbsp;nbsp;Requests the date of hardware release. The length of this product"
                "nbsp;nbsp;information is four bytes. Note: For example 30 November 2001 is 0x30"
                "nbsp;nbsp;0x11 0x20 0x01 (BCD) as a contiguous sequence having no spaces."
                "nbsp;nbsp;Also Note: In case of component variants with Flash memory instead of"
                "nbsp;nbsp;ROM this value shall denote the date of production."
                "- Product Bluetooth Country Code (LPRF_PROD_BT_COUNTRY_CODE):"
                "nbsp;nbsp;Requests the Bluetooth related Country information, which defines the valid"
                "nbsp;nbsp;range of frequency hopping scheme. The length of this product information"
                "nbsp;nbsp;is one byte. More detailed information concerning the Bluetooth country"
                "nbsp;nbsp;code can be found in the 'Host Controller Interface Functional Specification'"
                "nbsp;nbsp;part of the Bluetooth Specification[2]."
                "- Product Manufacturer (PROD_MANUFACTURER):"
                "nbsp;nbsp;Requests the ASCII string of the manufacturer name."
            enum
            {
                {0x0C, "LPRF_PROD_HW"},
                {0x0D, "LPRF_PROD_CODE"},
                {0x0E, "LPRF_PROD_CODE_BASIC"},
                {0x40, "LPRF_HW_RELEASE_DAY_MONTH_YEAR"},
                {0x13, "LPRF_PROD_BT_COUNTRY_CODE"},
                {0x43, "PROD_MANUFACTORER"}
            }
            present_if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x05);

        PARAM "Destination"
            uint8
            doc     "Fixed Destiation"
            default = 0x19
            present_if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x05);

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # HCI_ext_opcode = 0x85, "LOC_SET_HOST_WAKE_UP"
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        PARAM "Port Value"
            uint8
            doc "This HCI extension command is used to set (output function) the port HOST_WAKEUP"
                "   during production test to predefined signals (high or low)."
                "During normal usage of the Bluetooth module this HCI extension command must not be"
                "   used. Otherwise the wakeup functionality between host and Bluetooth module is"
                "   confused."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "singal low"},
                {0x01, "singal high"}
            }
            present_if ("HCI_ext_opcode" = 0x85);

            #   "During normal usage of the Bluetooth module this HCI extension command must not be"

            #   "confused."

            uint16 big_endian

            max = 4

        # ---------------------------------------
    }

    COMMAND "BCS_Debug_Config" 0x004
    {
        devs "*"=1
        doc "BCS Debug Config"

                {0x00000002, "SPI"}

            uint32

            bitmap
            {

                {0x00000004, "ERROR_CHECK"},

                {0x00000010, "CLOCK_UTIL"},
                {0x00000020, "TASK_API"},
                {0x00000040, "FREQ"},
                {0x00000080, "TIMING"},
                {0x00000100, "RESYNC"},
                {0x00000200, "HW_SCHEDULER"},
                {0x00000400, "HW_EVENTS"},
                {0x00000800, "RSSI"},
                {0x00001000, "COEX"},
                {0x00002000, "PROFILER"},

                {0x00008000, "LRM"},

                {0x00200000, "LE_INIT"},

                {0x00800000, "LE_ADV"},

    COMMAND "Super_Peek_Poke" 0x00A
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "Allows the RF registers and memory space (XDATA space for 8051-based chips) to be read"
            "or written. The parameters present are dependent on what memory space is being read or"
            "written and whether the access is a read or a write."
        PARAM "Access_Type"
            uint8
            doc "Selects which memory or register space type is to be read or written, and whether it"
                "is to be read or written."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "8051_XDATA_Peek"},
                {0x01, "BlueRF_Peek"},
                {0x02, "BlueRF_Poke"},
                {0x03, "8051_XDATA_Poke"},
                {0x04, "ARM_Memory_Peek"},
                {0x05, "ARM_Memory_Poke"},
                {0x06, "PMU_Peek"},
                {0x07, "PMU_Poke"}
            };
        PARAM "XDATA_Address"
            uint16 big_endian
            doc "Present for XDATA reads and writes on 8051-based chips. Specifies the XDATA address"
                "to be read or written."
            present_if (("Access_Type" = 0x00) or ("Access_Type" = 0x03));
        PARAM "BlueRF_Address"
            uint8
            doc "Present for RF register reads and writes. Specifies the RF register address to be"
                "read or written."
            present_if (("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x02));
        PARAM "ARM_Memory_Address"
            uint32
            doc "Present for memory reads and writes on ARM-based chips. Specifies the memory address"
                "to be read or written. For writes, the memory address must fall on a 32-bit"
                "address boundary."
            present_if (("Access_Type" = 0x04) or ("Access_Type" = 0x05));
        PARAM "PMU_Reg_Offset"
            uint8
            doc "Present for PMU register reads and writes. Specifies the PMU register address to be"
                "read or written."
            present_if (("Access_Type" = 0x06) or ("Access_Type" = 0x07));
        PARAM "Value8"
            uint8
            doc "Present for XDATA writes on 8051-based chips, or RF or PMU register writes on all chips."
                "Specifies the value to be written."
            present_if (    ("Access_Type" = 0x02) or ("Access_Type" = 0x03) or
                    ("Access_Type" = 0x07) );
        PARAM "Value32"
            uint32
            doc "Present for memory writes on ARM-based chips. Specifies the value to be written."
            present_if      ("Access_Type" = 0x05);
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
        CC_PARAM "Value"
            uint8
            doc "The value read, which is only valid if the access type was a read.";
    }

    COMMAND "Super_Duper_Peek_Poke" 0x00C
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2035"=0, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
            doc   "Allows the RF registers and memory space to be read or written."
            "The parameters present are dependent on what memory space is being read or written and whether the access is a read or a write."
            PARAM "Access_Type"
            uint8
            doc   "Selects which memory or register space type is to be read or written, and whether it is to be read or written."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "BlueRF_Peek"},
                {0x01, "BlueRF_Peek_Multi"},
                {0x02, "BlueRF_Poke"},
                {0x03, "ARM_8bit_Memory_Peek"},
                {0x04, "ARM_8bit_Memory_Peek_Multi"},
                {0x05, "ARM_8bit_Memory_Poke"},
                {0x06, "ARM_32bit_Memory_Peek"},
                {0x07, "ARM_32bit_Memory_Peek_Multi"},
                {0x08, "ARM_32bit_Memory_Poke"},
                {0x09, "ARM_8bit_Fifo_Peek_Multi"},
                {0x0A, "ARM_8bit_Fifo_Poke_Multi"},
                {0x0B, "ARM_16bit_Memory_Peek"}
            };
        PARAM "Address"
            uint32
            doc "The address to be read or written.";
        PARAM "Count"
            uint8
            doc "The number of values to be read or written, starting at Address."
            min = 1
            max = if ("Access_Type" = 0x09)  then    240
            else if ("Access_Type" = 0x0A) then 240
                else 60
            #max = 60
            enabled_if( ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x04) or ("Access_Type" = 0x07) or ("Access_Type"=0x09) or ("Access_Type" = 0x0A) );
        PARAM "Value"
            uint32
            doc   "For poke operations, the value to be written."
            max = if ("Access_Type" = 0x02)  then    255
            else if ("Access_Type" = 0x05) then 255
                else 0xffffffff
                    present_if( ("Access_Type" = 0x02) or ("Access_Type" = 0x05) or ("Access_Type" = 0x08) );
        PARAM "Data"
           uint8[240] omit_pad_bytes
           doc "The data to write to the target 8 bits fifo address."
           min = 1
           valid_length = ByteArrayValidLength("Data")
           present_if("Access_Type" = 0x0A );
                CC_PARAM "Status"
                    errorcode8;
                CC_PARAM "Access_Type"
                    uint8
                    doc "The type of peek or poke was processed.";
                CC_PARAM "Address"
                    uint32
                    doc "The address or starting address that was read or written.";
                CC_PARAM "Count"
                    uint8
                    doc "The number of values returned."
                    "For pokes or single (as opposed to multiple) peeks, the value will be irrelevant,"
                    "but faithfully indicated as being 1."
                    min = 1
                    max = if ("Access_Type" = 0x09)  then    240
                          else if ("Access_Type" = 0x0A) then 240
                          else 60;
                CC_PARAM "Data"
           uint8[240] omit_pad_bytes
           doc "The data read from the target 8bits fifo."
           min = 1
           valid_length = ByteArrayValidLength("Data")
           present_if("Access_Type" = 0x09 );

                CC_PARAM "Value[0]"
                    uint32
                    doc "The value that was read from the specified address.  If an 8-bit read was processed, the value will be padded with zeros to a big endian 32-bit value.  To read RAM in a more compact form with the capacity for a greater length, use Read_RAM instead."
                    present_if( ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x02) or ("Access_Type" = 0x03) or ("Access_Type" = 0x04) or ("Access_Type" = 0x05) or ("Access_Type" = 0x06) or ("Access_Type" = 0x07) or ("Access_Type" = 0x08) or ("Access_Type" = 0x0A) or ("Access_Type" = 0x0B));
                CC_PARAM "Value[1]"
                    uint32
                    present_if( ( ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x04) or ("Access_Type" = 0x07) ) and ("Count" > 1) );
                CC_PARAM "Value[2]"
                    uint32
                    present_if( ( ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x04) or ("Access_Type" = 0x07) ) and ("Count" > 2) );
                CC_PARAM "Value[3]"
                    uint32
                    present_if( ( ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x04) or ("Access_Type" = 0x07) ) and ("Count" > 3) );
                CC_PARAM "Value[4]"
                    uint32
                    present_if( ( ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x04) or ("Access_Type" = 0x07) ) and ("Count" > 4) );
                CC_PARAM "Value[5]"
                    uint32
                    present_if( ( ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x04) or ("Access_Type" = 0x07) ) and ("Count" > 5) );

    # COMMAND "Read_Park_Parameters" 0x00D
    COMMAND "BT/LE Connection RSSI History" 0x00D
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM4349"=1
        doc "Reset,Enable,Disable,Read BT/LE rssi history"
        PARAM "RSSI_History_Auto_Report"
            bool8
            doc "Send RSSI History event automatically";

        PARAM "RSSI_History_Action"
            uint8
            doc "Action"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Reset all tracking"},
                {0x01, "Enable on Connection Handle"},
                {0x02, "Disable on Connection Handle"},
                {0x03, "Read buffer on Connection Handle"}
            }
            default = 0x01;

        PARAM "RSSI_History_ConnHandle"
            connhandle16
            doc "Connection Handle"
            present_if ( ("RSSI_History_Action" = 1) or ("RSSI_History_Action" = 2) or ("RSSI_History_Action" = 3) );

        PARAM "RSSI_History_BufSize"
            uint16
            doc "Buffer Size in Bytes"
            min = 6
# We don't restricte the max allowed size in Bluetool RSSI History command, because
# RSSI History command (VSC:0xD) uses UINT16 for buffer size instead of UINT8.
# We DO have restriction on total memory allowed for RSSI History tracking by a global configurable variable.
#           max = 246 # We don't restricte the max allowed buffer size in Bluetool RSSI History command
            present_if ( "RSSI_History_Action" = 1 );

        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;

        CC_PARAM "Auto_Report"
            bool8
            doc "Auto report RSSI History enabled.";

        CC_PARAM "Available_Memory"
            uint16
            doc "Return available memory";

        CC_PARAM "Action"
            uint8
            doc "Action"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Reset all tracking"},
                {0x01, "Enable on Connection Handle"},
                {0x02, "Disable on Connection Handle"},
                {0x03, "Read buffer on Connection Handle"}
            };

        CC_PARAM "Data_Length"
            uint8
            doc "Number of rssi data"
            min = 0
            max = 246 # the max size of RSSI History data CC can carry
            present_if ("Action" = 3);

        CC_PARAM "RSSI[0]"
            uint8
            doc "RSSI history data in compact format"
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 0) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[1]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 1) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[2]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 2) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[3]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 3) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[4]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 4) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[5]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 5) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[6]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 6) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[7]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 7) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[8]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 8) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[9]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 9) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[10]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 10) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[11]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 11) );

        CC_PARAM "RSSI[15]"

            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 15) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[16]"
            uint8
            present_if ( ("Action" = 3) and ("Data_Length" > 16) );
        CC_PARAM "RSSI[17]"

    COMMAND "Write_Hopping_Channels" 0x012
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2035"=1, "BCM2040"=1, "BCM2042"=1
        doc "This command will set frequency hopping to 79 channel, 23 channel"
            "or fixed for all connections.  Also adjust the maximum transmit"
            "power for all connections."

        # BINARY MESSAGE PARAMETERS, NOT IN USER INTERFACE OR SCRIPT EVENTS:
        PARAM "Hopping_Change"
            uint8
            doc "0 - No change, ignore Hopping_Channels_Binary<br>"
                "1 - Change to 79 or 23 hops<br>"
                "2 - Change to fixed frequency"
            binary_message_only
            encode_value =
                if      ("Hopping_Channels" = 0) then       1
                else if ("Hopping_Channels" = 1) then       1
                else if ("Hopping_Channels" = 0xFF) then    0
                else                                        2;
        PARAM "Hopping_Channels_Binary"
            uint8
            doc "If Hopping_Change is change to 79 or 23 hops:<br>"
                "&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; 0x02 - Change to 79 hops<br>"
                "&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; 0xB6 - Change to 23 hops<br>"
                "<br>"
                "If Hopping_Change is change to fixed frequency:<br>"
                "&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; 0x02 - Change to 2402 MHz<br>"
                "&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; ...<br>"
                "&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; 0x50 - Change to 2480 MHz"

                if      ("Hopping_Channels" = 0) then       0xB6
                else if ("Hopping_Channels" = 1) then       0x02

                else                                        "Hopping_Channels";

            decode_value =
                if      ("Hopping_Change" = 0) then             0xFF

                else                                            1;

        # POWER ADJUSTMENT PARAMETERS

    COMMAND "Set_Tx_Carrier_Frequency_ARM" 0x014
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2035"=0, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "For 2045, put radio into manual mode for tx tests"
        PARAM "Carrier_Enable"
            uint8
            doc "Used to turn the carrier on and off"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Carrier on"},
                {0x01, "Carrier off"}
            };
        PARAM "Carrier_Frequency_Encoded"
            uint8
            doc "If Carrier_Enable is 0, Bluetooth frequency minus 2400"
            binary_message_only
            encode_value =  if ("Carrier_Enable" = 0) then  ("Carrier_Frequency" - 2400)
                            else        2;
        PARAM "Carrier_Frequency"
            uint16
            doc "An abstraction of Carrier_Frequency_Encoded as Bluetooth frequency in Mhz"
            min = 2402
            max = 2490
            note "MHz"
            not_in_binary_message
            decode_value = ("Carrier_Frequency_Encoded" + 2400)
            enabled_if ("Carrier_Enable" = 0);
        PARAM "Mode"
            uint8
            doc "If Carrier_Enable is 0, selects unmodulated or modulated with pattern"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Unmodulated"},
                {0x01, "PRBS9"},
                {0x02, "PRBS15"},
                {0x03, "All Zeros"},
                {0x04, "All Ones"},
                {0x05, "Incrementing Symbols"}
            }
            enabled_if ("Carrier_Enable" = 0);
        PARAM "Modulation Type"
            uint8
            doc "If Carrier_Enable is 0, 1 Mbps, 2Mbps, or 3 Mbps selection, ignored if Mode is"
                "Unmodulated"

            {
                {0x00, "GFSK"},
                {0x01, "QPSK"},
                {0x02, "8PSK"},
                {0x03, "LE"}

            enabled_if ( ("Carrier_Enable" = 0) and ("Mode" > 0) );
        PARAM "Transmit_Power"
            uint8
            doc "If Carrier_Enable is 0, requested power table index"
                        enum

                            {0x00, "0 dBm"},
                            {0x01, "-4 dBm"},
                            {0x02, "-8 dBm"},
                            {0x03, "-12 dBm"},
                            {0x04, "-16 dBm"},
                            {0x05, "-20 dBm"},
                            {0x06, "-24 dBm"},
                            {0x07, "-28 dBm"},
                            {0x08, "Specify Power in dBm"},
                            {0x09, "Specify Power Table index"}
                        }
                        enabled_if ("Carrier_Enable" = 0);
                PARAM "Transmit_Power_dBm"
                        int8
                        doc "If Carrier_Enable is 0 and Transmit_Power is Integer override, requested output power in dbm"
                        note "dBm"
                        enabled_if (("Carrier_Enable" = 0) and ("Transmit_Power" = 8));
                PARAM "Transmit_Power_Table_Index"
                        uint8
                        doc "If Carrier_Enable is 0 and Transmit_Power is Table index override, requested table index"
                        max = 7
                        enabled_if (("Carrier_Enable" = 0) and ("Transmit_Power" = 9));
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
    }

    COMMAND "FM_RDS_Command" 0x015
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2048"=1
        doc "This command encapsulates a variety of FM_RDS I2C bus transactions."
        PARAM "I2C_Address"
            uint8
            doc "The register to be addressed."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "I2C_FM_RDS_SYSTEM"},
                {0x01, "I2C_FM_CTRL"},
                {0x02, "I2C_RDS_CTRL"},
                {0x04, "I2C_FM_AUDIO_PAUSE"},
                {0x05, "I2C_FM_AUDIO_CTRL"},
                {0x07, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_CTRL"},
                {0x08, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_CTRL1"},
                {0x09, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_TUNE_MODE"},
                {0x0A, "I2C_FM_FREQ"},
                {0x0C, "I2C_FM_AF_FREQ"},
                {0x0E, "I2C_FM_CARRIER"},
                {0x0F, "I2C_FM_RSSI"},
                {0x12, "I2C_FM_RDS_FLAG"},
                {0x10, "I2C_FM_RDS_MASK"},
                {0x14, "I2C_RDS_WLINE"},
                {0x16, "I2C_RDS_BLKB_MATCH"},
                {0x18, "I2C_RDS_BLKB_MASK"},
                {0x1A, "I2C_RDS_PI_MATCH"},
                {0x1C, "I2C_RDS_PI_MASK"},
                {0x80, "I2C_RDS_DATA"},
                {0x1E, "I2C_FM_RDS_BOOT"},
                {0x1F, "I2C_FM_RDS_TEST"},
                {0x29, "I2C_SLAVE_CONFIG"},
                {0x4D, "I2C_FM_ROUTE_PCM"},
                {0x90, "I2C_FM_BEST_TUNE_MODE"},
                {0xDA, "I2C_FM_SMUTE_VERSION3"},
                {0xDB, "I2C_FM_FEATURES"},
                {0xdE, "I2C_FM_PRESCAN_QUALITY"},
                {0xdF, "I2C_FM_SNR"},
                {0xF5, "FM_I2C_ADDITIONAL_AUIDO_PATH"},
                {0xF6, "I2C_FM_ANTENNA_MATCHING"},
                {0xF8, "I2C_FM_VOLUME_CTRL"},
                {0xF9, "I2C_FM_STEREO_BLEND_SOFT_MUTE"},
                {0xFA, "I2C_FM_ANTENNA_SELECTION"},
                {0xFB, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_BOUNDARY"},
                {0xFC, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_METHOD"},
                {0xFD, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_STEP"},
                {0xFE, "I2C_FM_PRESET_MAX_CHANNEL"},
                {0xFF, "I2C_FM_PRESET_CHANNEL"}
            };
        PARAM "Read_Write_Mode"
            uint8
            doc "Whether the register is being read or written."
            enum
            {
                {0, "Write"},
                {1, "Read"}
            };
        PARAM "Read_Length"
            uint8
            doc "If Read_Write_Mode is 1 (Read), this parameter is included and indicates the"
                "length in bytes of data to be read, else it is excluded. This parameter is"
                "explicitly included for use in translation to an I2C bus transaction, but is fixed"
                "for a given I2C bus address."
            present_if ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1)
            binary_message_only
            encode_value =  if      ("Read_Byte_Count_Override" = 1) then "Read_Byte_Count"
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x00) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x01) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x02) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x04) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x05) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x07) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x08) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x09) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x0A) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x0C) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x0E) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x0F) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x12) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x10) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x14) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x16) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x18) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x1A) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x1C) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x80) then "Read_Byte_Count"
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x1E) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x1F) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x29) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x4D) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x90) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xDA) then 5
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xDB) then 4       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xDE) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xDF) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xF6) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xF8) then 2       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xF9) then 8       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFA) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFB) then 4       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFC) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFD) then 2       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFE) then 1       # TBD
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFF) then "Read_Byte_Count"
                            else                                255;    # Error
        PARAM "Read_Byte_Count_Override"
            bool8
            doc "An abstraction by which to override the byte count to read from any"
                "register."
            not_in_binary_message
            enabled_if ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1)
            decode_value =  if  ("I2C_Address" = 0x00)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x01)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x02)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x04)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x05)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x07)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x08)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x09)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x0A)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x0C)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x0E)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x0F)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x12)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x10)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x14)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x16)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x18)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x1A)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x1C)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x80) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x1E) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x1F) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x29) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x4D) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x90) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xDA) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xDB) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xDE) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xDF) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xF6) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xF8) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xF9) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFA) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFB) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFC) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFD) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFE) then 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xFF) then 1
                            else                                1;  # Error;

        PARAM "Read_Byte_Count"
            uint8
            doc "An abstraction by which to provide the byte count to read from the I2C_RDS_DATA"
                "register, or any other register if Read_Byte_Count_Override is enabled."
            not_in_binary_message
            enabled_if (    ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1)
                            and ( ("I2C_Address" = 0x80) or ("Read_Byte_Count_Override" = 1) ) )
            decode_value = "Read_Length"
            max = 250;

        ############################################################################################
        # Write access - I2C_FM_RDS_SYSTEM
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_RDS_SYSTEM_Flags"
            uint8
            doc "Enable or disable the FM and RDS functions."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x00) )
            bitmap
            {
                {0x01, "FM_ON"},
                {0x02, "RDS_ON"}
            };

        ############################################################################################

        ############################################################################################

                {0x01, "Band select"},
                {0x02, "Stereo/mono auto select"},
                {0x04, "Stereo/mono manual select"},
                {0x08, "Stereo/mono blend/switch"},

        PARAM "I2C_FM_AUDIO_CTRL_Audio_Bandwidth_Select"

            max = 3;

            int8

        ############################################################################################
        # Write access - I2C_FM_SEARCH_TUNE_MODE
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_SEARCH_TUNE_MODE_Flags"
            uint8
            doc "Selects the FM auto search/tune function in one of three modes.  Function stopped when"
                "bits cleared. Bits used immediately. State machine should be in stop state before"
                "starting new activity."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x09) )
            max = 3;

        ############################################################################################
        # Write access - I2C_FM_FREQ
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_FREQ"
            uint16
            doc "Sets the start or first frequency in the search or direct tune commands. Does not"
                "take effect until the tuner state machine is kicked off. 16-bit value representing"
                "the FM setting in 1 KHz units offset from 64 MHz."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x0A) )
            note "KHz offset from 64 MHz";

        ############################################################################################
        # Write access - I2C_FM_AF_FREQ
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_AF_FREQ"
            uint16
            doc "Sets the AF jump frequency. Does not take effect until the tuner state machine is"
                "kicked off. 16-bit value representing the desired FM setting in 1 KHz units offset"
                "from 64 MHz."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x0C) )
            note "KHz offset from 64 MHz";

        ############################################################################################
        # Write access - I2C_FM_RDS_MASK
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_RDS_MASK"
            uint16
            doc "Interrupt mask register for the FM interrupt line based on the contents of the"

            bitmap

                {0x0001, "SEARCH_TUNE_FINISHED"},
                {0x0002, "SEARCH_TUNE_FAIL"},

                {0x0010, "AUDIO_PAUSE_INDICATION"},
                {0x0020, "STEREO DETECTED"},
                {0x0040, "STEREO ACTIVE"},
                {0x0200, "FIFO_WLINE"},
                {0x0800, "B_BLOCK_MATCH"},
                {0x1000, "SYNC_LOST"},
                {0x2000, "PI_MATCH"},

            uint8

        ############################################################################################
        # Write access - I2C_FM_ROUTE_PCM
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_ROUTE_PCM"
            uint8
            doc "This command is used to control the fm audio route to PCM."
                "When the MSB is set, FM audio routes to PCM."
                "User should perform read, modify then write on this register"
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x4D) );


        ############################################################################################
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_BEST_TUNE_MODE"
            uint8
            doc "This command is used to enable/disable best tune; Only bit7 should be written;"
                "bit[7] = 0 enables best tune; bit[7] = 1  disable best tune."
                "This command need a read, modify then write."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x90) );

        ############################################################################################
        # Write access - I2C_FM_START_LOWPASS_FILTER_SNR

        PARAM "I2C_FM_START_LOWPASS_FILTER_SNR"

            doc "The PSD estimeate value at which we begin lowpass filtering, range -128 - 127;"

                        min = -128
                        max = 127;

        ############################################################################################

                        max = 127;

        ############################################################################################

            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xDA) )
                        min = -128
                        max = 127;

                        max = 127;
        ############################################################################################

                        max = 63;

        ############################################################################################

            uint8
            doc "This command is used for FM RX antenna selection;"
                " 0x0 means internal antenna; 0x1 means external antenna."

        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_SEARCH_UPPER_BOUNDARY"
            uint16
            doc "This command is used to set the upper search boundary. It is in 1 KHz unit offset from 64MHz; This need to be programmed with the lower boundary together to setup the new search range."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xFB) );

        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_SEARCH_LOWER_BOUNDARY"
            uint16
            doc "This command is used to set the lower search boundary. It is in 1 KHz unit offset from 64MHz; This need to be programmed with the upper boundary together to setup the new search range."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xFB) );

        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_SEARCH_METHOD"
            uint8
            doc "This command is used to set the search method; 0 = normal search; 1 = preset search; 2 = rssi search."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xFC) );

        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FM_SEARCH_STEP"
            uint16

            uint8
            doc "This command is used to control the fm audio route to PCM."

        ############################################################################################
        # Command Complete event
        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
        CC_PARAM "I2C_Address"
            uint8
            doc "The register to be addressed."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "I2C_FM_RDS_SYSTEM"},
                {0x01, "I2C_FM_CTRL"},
                {0x02, "I2C_RDS_CTRL"},
                {0x04, "I2C_FM_AUDIO_PAUSE"},

                {0x07, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_CTRL"},
                {0x08, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_CTRL1"},
                {0x09, "I2C_FM_SEARCH_TUNE_MODE"},
                {0x0A, "I2C_FM_FREQ"},
                {0x0C, "I2C_FM_AF_FREQ"},
                {0x0E, "I2C_FM_CARRIER"},
                {0x0F, "I2C_FM_RSSI"},
                {0x12, "I2C_FM_RDS_FLAG"},
                {0x10, "I2C_FM_RDS_MASK"},
                {0x14, "I2C_RDS_WLINE"},

                {0x1A, "I2C_RDS_PI_MATCH"},
                {0x1C, "I2C_RDS_PI_MASK"},

                {0xDA, "I2C_FM_SMUTE_VERSION3"},
                {0xDB, "I2C_FM_FEATURES"},
                {0xDE, "I2C_FM_PRESCAN_QUALITY"},

                {0xF8, "I2C_FM_VOLUME_CTRL"},
                {0xF9, "I2C_FM_STEREO_BLEND_SOFT_MUTE"},

                {0xFE, "I2C_FM_PRESET_MAX_CHANNEL"},

            };

            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x0C) )
            note "KHz offset from 64 MHz";

        ############################################################################################
        # Read access - I2C_FM_CARRIER

            doc "Carrier frequency error (from baseband PLL) in 1 KHz increments. Error term from"
                "nominal IF."

        ############################################################################################

        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "I2C_FM_RDS_FLAG"
            uint16
            doc "Status bits used to possibly assert a single level triggered FM interrupt line."
                "All bits are cleared after with the associated register read.  Mask bits are also"
                "cleared when register read."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x12) )
            bitmap
            {
                {0x0001, "SEARCH_TUNE_FINISHED"},
                {0x0002, "SEARCH_TUNE_FAIL"},
                {0x0004, "RSSI_LOW"},
                {0x0008, "CARRIER_ERROR_HIGH"},
                {0x0010, "AUDIO_PAUSE_INDICATION"},
                {0x0020, "STEREO DETECTED"},
                {0x0040, "STEREO ACTIVE"},
                {0x0200, "FIFO_WLINE"},
                {0x0800, "B_BLOCK_MATCH"},
                {0x1000, "SYNC_LOST"},
                {0x2000, "PI_MATCH"},
                {0x4000, "RDS_bit_flag_transition"}

    COMMAND "Set_Encryption_Key_Size" 0x016
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "This command sets local device acceptable encryption key sizes."
        PARAM "Key_Size"
            uint16
            doc "Key Size Tier Word specifying acceptable key sizes as"
                "bit positions, e.g. 0x9001 means acceptable key sizes"
                "are 16, 13, and 1."
            bitmap
            {
                {0x0001,"Accept 8-bit key"},
                {0x0002,"Accept 16-bit key"},
                {0x0004,"Accept 24-bit key"},
                {0x0008,"Accept 32-bit key"},
                {0x0010,"Accept 40-bit key"},
                {0x0020,"Accept 48-bit key"},
                {0x0040,"Accept 56-bit key"},
                {0x0080,"Accept 64-bit key"},
                {0x0100,"Accept 72-bit key"},
                {0x0200,"Accept 80-bit key"},
                {0x0400,"Accept 88-bit key"},
                {0x0800,"Accept 96-bit key"},
                {0x1000,"Accept 104-bit key"},
                {0x2000,"Accept 112-bit key"},
                {0x4000,"Accept 120-bit key"},
                {0x8000,"Accept 128-bit key"}
            };
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
    }

    COMMAND "Invalidate_Flash_And_Reboot" 0x017
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2035"=1
        doc "This command invalidates and erases supported FLASH parts"
            "if installed. Thereafter the device will be automatically rebooted.<br>"
            "Invalidate flash and reboot command now causes the following actions:<br>"
            "1) System w/ Flash<br>"
            " - return Successful Command Status event<br>"
            "1.1)  Attempt to Erase Flash Successful<br>"
            " - return Successful Command Complete Event then go Reboot<br>"
            "1.2)  Attempt to Erase Flash Failed<br>"
            " - return Hardware Failure Command Complete Event then go Reboot<br>"
            "2) System w/o Flash<br>"
            " - return Successful Command Complete Event then go Reboot.<br>"
            "In the event that this is a USB device, toggle D-lines and"
            "re-enumerate to hardware default VID/PID values.  This will"
            "signal the host that a repeat of the firmware download procedure is required."
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
    }

    COMMAND "Update_UART_Baud_Rate" 0x018
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "Changes the baud rate at which the UART transport communicates. A Command Complete"
            "event will be generated at the previous baud rate. If the Command Complete event"
            "indicates success, the UART transport will then shift to the new baud rate."
        PARAM "Encoded_Baud_Rate"
            uint16
            doc "The hardware register representation of the new baud rate. The value is determined"
                "by a formula described below.  For the BCM2045, the value of Encoded_Baud_Rate can"
                "be 0x0000, which implies that the encoded form is not used and the actual baud rate"
                "follows.  When using the UART transport of the BCM2045 in three-wire SLIP (H5)"
                "mode, if Update_UART_Baud_Rate is issued, use of the encoded form is disallowed. If"
                "the encoded form is used, the value is determined as follows:<br>"
                "<br>"
                "<pre>"
                "// Upper four bits<br>"
                "uint32 encoded_segment = ( ( (24000000 / baud_rate) % 16 ) / 2 );<br>"
                "encoded_baud_rate = ( ( (uint16)encoded_segment ) << 4 );<br>"
                "<br>"
                "// Lower four bits of high nibble<br>"
                "encoded_segment = ( ( ( (24000000 / baud_rate) % 16 ) / 2 ) +<br>"
                "&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;"
                "&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;"
                "( ( (24000000 / baud_rate) % 16 ) % 2 ) );<br>"
                "encoded_baud_rate |= (uint16)encoded_segment;<br>"
                "<br>"
                "// Lower byte<br>"
                "encoded_segment = ( 256 - ( (24000000 / baud_rate) / 16 ) );<br>"
                "encoded_baud_rate |= (encoded_segment << 8);<br>"
                "</pre>"
                "Note: the above formula is only based on 24 MHz UART clock."
                "For the BCM2048, we could select either 24MHz or 48MHz, so the above calculate will"
                "not work with 48MHz UART clock."
            binary_message_only
            encode_value =  if ("Use_Encoded_Form" = 0) then    0
                            else                                EncodeBaudRate("Baud_Rate");
        PARAM "Use_Encoded_Form"
            bool8
            doc "An abstraction of whether the encoded form is to be used. If not using the encoded"
                "form of baud rate, the Encoded_Baud_Rate parameter will be 0x0000, and a four-byte"
                "baud rate in integer form will follow in the Explicit_Baud_Rate parameter."
            not_in_binary_message
            decode_value =  if ("Encoded_Baud_Rate" = 0) then   0
                            else                                1;
        PARAM "Explicit_Baud_Rate"
            baudrate32
            doc "If Encoded_Baud_Rate is 0x0000 and therefore the encoded form of baud rate is not"
                "being used, the Explicit_Baud_Rate parameter will be present, containing the baud"
                "rate in integer form."
            binary_message_only
            present_if ("Use_Encoded_Form" = 0)
            min = 0
            max = 8000000
            encode_value = "Baud_Rate";
        PARAM "Baud_Rate"
            baudrate32
            doc "An abstraction of the baud rate, whether present in the command in encoded form in"
                "the Encoded_Baud_Rate or included as Explicit_Baud_Rate."
            not_in_binary_message
            min = 0
            max =   if ("Use_Encoded_Form" = 0) then    4000000
                    else                                1500000
            decode_value =  if ("Use_Encoded_Form" = 0) then    "Explicit_Baud_Rate"
                            else                                DecodeBaudRate("Encoded_Baud_Rate");
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
    }

    COMMAND "GPIO_Config_and_Write" 0x019
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2045"=1
        doc "This command config the pad of a selected GPIO and set its value"
            "if it was set as an output"
        PARAM "Auxiliary_GPIO_Control"
            bool in uint8{7}
            doc "If true, the auxiliary GPIOs will be configured.";
        PARAM "GPIO_Pin_Number"
            uint8{6:0}
            doc "GPIO pin number"
            max = 7;
        PARAM "Pad_Config"
            uint8
            doc "Configures the pad setting bits"
            bitmap
            {
                {0x01, "Input"},
                {0x02, "Pullup"},
                {0x04, "Pulldown"},
                {0x08, "Hysteresis"},
                {0x10, "Sel0"},
                {0x20, "Sel1"}
            };
        PARAM "GPIO_Set_Value"
            uint8
            doc "GPIO set value, can be high or low if the pad is set as an output"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Low"},
                {0x01, "High"}
            }
            enabled_if( ("Pad_Config" & 1) = 0 );

        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
        CC_PARAM "Auxiliary_GPIO_Control"
            bool in uint8{7}
            doc "If true, the auxiliary GPIOs will be configured.";
        CC_PARAM "GPIO_Pin_Number"
            uint8{6:0}
            doc "GPIO pin number"
            max = 7;
        CC_PARAM "GPIO_Value"
            uint8
            doc "Whether the GPIO is high or low. If the pin is set to be an output, the selected"
                "high/low value setting. If the pin is set to be an input, the current high/low"
                "value of that pin."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Low"},
                {0x01, "High"}
            };
        CC_PARAM "Pad_Config"
            uint8
            doc "Configuration of the pad setting bits"
            bitmap
            {
                {0x01, "Input"},
                {0x02, "Pullup"},
                {0x04, "Pulldown"},
                {0x08, "Hysteresis"},

                {0x00, "PCM"},
                {0x01, "Transport"},
                {0x02, "CODEC"},
                {0x03, "I2S"}
            }
            default = 1;
        PARAM "PCM_Interface_Rate"
            uint8

            enum
            {
                {0x00, "128 KBps"},
                {0x01, "256 KBps"},
                {0x02, "512 KBps"},
                {0x03, "1024 KBps"},
                {0x04, "2048 KBps"}
            };
        PARAM "Frame_Type"
            uint8
            doc "Specifies the PCM frame type: short frame or long frame"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Short"},
                {0x01, "Long"}
            };
        PARAM "Sync_Mode"
            uint8
            doc "Specifies whether Bluetooth module to be the master or slave for PCM_SYNC signal"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Slave"},
                {0x01, "Master"}
            };
        PARAM "Clock_Mode"
            uint8
            doc "Specifies whether Bluetooth module to be the master or slave for PCM_CLK signal"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Slave"},
                {0x01, "Master"}
            };
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
    }

    COMMAND "Write_Comfort_Noise_Param" 0x020
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2035"=1
        doc "This command will write SCO comfort noise parameters. Comfort noise is used to"
            "lessen the audible effects of dropped packets in a SCO data stream."
        PARAM "Gain"
            uint8
            doc "A zero value disables comfort noise.  A nonzero value enables comfort noise, and"
                "determines the amplitude of samples in the random noise insertion phase as"
                "described in the Insert_Comfort_Noise_Count parameter documentation.";
        PARAM "Repeat_Last_Sample_Count"
            uint8
            doc "When an expected packet is not received, the last sample will be repeated this"
                "number of times or until another packet is received, whichever comes first.";
        PARAM "Insert_Comfort_Noise_Count"
            uint8
            doc "If after Repeat_Last_Sample_Count samples, no more packets are received, then"
                "Insert_Comfort_Noise_Count random samples will be inserted.  The amplitude of the"
                "random samples is determined by the Gain parameter.  For 0 < Gain < 8, the"
                "amplitude is 2^Gain. For example, if Gain=7, the amplitude of the random samples"
                "is -128/+127.  If, after Insert_Comfort_Noise_Count samples, still no more packets"
                "are received, zeros will be inserted indefinitely until another packet is"
                "received.";
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
    }

    COMMAND "Read_Comfort_Noise_Param" 0x021
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2035"=1
        doc "This command will read SCO comfort noise parameters. Comfort noise is used to"
            "lessen the audible effects of dropped packets in a SCO data stream."
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
        CC_PARAM "Gain"
            uint8
            doc "A zero value disables comfort noise.  A nonzero value enables comfort noise, and"
                "determines the amplitude of samples in the random noise insertion phase as"
                "described in the Insert_Comfort_Noise_Count parameter documentation.";
        CC_PARAM "Repeat_Last_Sample_Count"
            uint8
            doc "When an expected packet is not received, the last sample will be repeated this"
                "number of times or until another packet is received, whichever comes first.";
        CC_PARAM "Insert_Comfort_Noise_Count"
            uint8
            doc "If after Repeat_Last_Sample_Count samples, no more packets are received, then"
                "Insert_Comfort_Noise_Count random samples will be inserted.  The amplitude of the"
                "random samples is determined by the Gain parameter.  For 0 < Gain < 8, the"
                "amplitude is 2^Gain. For example, if Gain=7, the amplitude of the random samples"
                "is -128/+127.  If, after Insert_Comfort_Noise_Count samples, still no more packets"
                "are received, zeros will be inserted indefinitely until another packet is"
                "received.";
    }

    COMMAND "Set_Sleepmode_Param" 0x027
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "This command will activate the selected sleep mode algorithm"
            "and specify respective timer thresholds."
        PARAM "Sleep_Mode"
            uint8
            doc "Sleep mode algorithm selection."
            enum
            {
                {0, "No sleep mode"},
                {1, "UART"},
                {2, "UART with messaging"},
                {3, "USB"},
                {4, "H4IBSS"},
                {5, "USB with host wake"},
                {6, "SDIO"},
                {7, "UART CS-N"},
                {8, "SPI"},
                {9, "H5"},
                {10, "H4DS"},
                {12, "UART with BREAK"}
            };
        PARAM "Idle_Threshold_Host"
            uint8
            doc "Host Idle Threshold, applicable To Sleep Modes 1, 2, 5, 7."
                "This is the number of firmware loops executed with no activity"
                "before the Host wake line is deasserted. Activity includes HCI"
                "traffic excluding certain sleep mode commands and the presence"
                "of SCO connections if the \"Allow Host Sleep During SCO\" flag is"
                "not set to 1. Each count of this parameter is roughly equivalent"
                "to 300 ms. For example, when this parameter is set to 16 (0x10),"
                "the Host wake line will be deasserted after approximately 4.8"
                "seconds of inactivity."
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 1) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 2) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 5) or
                        ("Sleep_Mode" = 7) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 12));
        PARAM "Idle_Threshold_HC"
            uint8
            doc "Host Controller Idle Threshold, applicable To Sleep Modes 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6,"
                "7 and 9. This is the number of firmware loops executed with no activity"
                "before the HC is considered idle. Depending on the mode, HC may"
                "then attempt to sleep. Activity includes HCI traffic excluding"
                "certain sleep mode commands and the presence of ACL/SCO connections."
                "Each count of this parameter is roughly equivalent to 300 ms."
                "For example, when this parameter is set to 16 (0x10), the HC will"
                "be considered idle after approximately 4.8 seconds of inactivity."
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 1) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 2) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 3) or
                        ("Sleep_Mode" = 4) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 5) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 6) or
                        ("Sleep_Mode" = 7) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 9) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 12));
        PARAM "BT_WAKE_Active_Mode"
            uint8
            doc "Applicable To Sleep Modes 1, 2, 7."
                "This flag indicates whether the BT_WAKE line is active low or high."
                "GPIO0 is typically used for BT_WAKE."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Active Low"},
                {0x01, "Active High"}
            }
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 1) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 2) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 7) or
                        ("Sleep_Mode" = 12));
        PARAM "HOST_WAKE_Active_Mode"
            uint8
            doc "Applicable To Sleep Modes 1, 2, 5, 7."
                "This flag indicates whether the HOST_WAKE line is active low or high."
                "GPIO3 is typically used for HOST_WAKE on BCM2035, and GPIO1 on ARM-based chips."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Active Low"},
                {0x01, "Active High"}
            }
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 1) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 2) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 5) or
                        ("Sleep_Mode" = 7));
        PARAM "Allow_Host_Sleep_During_SCO"
            bool8
            doc "Applicable To Sleep Modes 1, 2, 3, 5, 7."
                "When this flag is set to 0, the host is not allowed to sleep while"
                "an SCO is active. In modes 1 and 2, the device will keep the host"
                "wake line asserted while an SCO is active. In mode 3, the device"
                "will immediately issue a USB RESUME if the host issues a SUSPEND."
                "When this flag is set to 1, the host can sleep while an SCO is active."
                "This flag should only be set to 1 if SCO traffic is directed to the PCM interface."
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 1) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 2) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 3) or
                        ("Sleep_Mode" = 5) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 7));
        PARAM "Combine_Sleep_Mode_And_LPM"
            bool8
            doc "Applicable To Sleep Modes 1, 2, 3, 5, 7."
                "In Mode 0, always set byte 7 to 0. In all sleep modes, device always"
                "requires permission to sleep between scans / periodic inquiries regardless"
                "of the setting of this byte. In Modes 1 and 2, if byte is set, device must"
                "have \"permission\" to sleep during the low power modes of sniff, hold, and park."
                "If byte is not set, device can sleep without permission during these modes."
                "Permission to sleep in Mode 1 is obtained if the BT_WAKE signal is not asserted."
                "Permission to sleep in Mode 2 occurs after the Sleep Request / Sleep Request ACK exchange."
                "In Mode 3 and 5, if the byte is set to 0, the device will not be able to sleep"
                "during the low power modes. If it is set to 1, the device will be able so sleep"
                "during the low power modes."
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 1) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 2) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 3) or
                        ("Sleep_Mode" = 5) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 7));
        PARAM "Enable_Tristate_Control_Of_UART_Tx_Line"
            bool8
            doc "Applicable To Sleep Modes 1, 2, 7."
                "When set to 0, the device will not tristate its UART TX line before going to sleep.<br>"
                "When set to 1, the device will tristate its UART TX line before going to sleep."
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 1) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 2) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 7));
        PARAM "Active_Connection_Handling_On_Suspend"
            uint8
            doc "Suspend Behavior, applicable To Sleep Modes 3, 5."
                "When set to 0, this flag indicates that upon detecting a USB suspend,"
                "the device should sleep whenever possible. This means that the device"
                "will stay up if necessary to maintain active ACL and/or SCO connections,"
                "and will wake up to perform any scheduled periodic activities if configured to do so."
                "When set to 1, the device will immediate go to sleep upon detecting a USB"
                "SUSPEND and will not wake up until USB RESUME is detected. This will cause"
                "all connections (whether ACL or SCO, parked or not, etc.) to be dropped."
                "All periodic activity will also be suspended. When this flag is set to 1,"
                "all other parameters are ignored."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Maintain connections; sleep when timed activity allows"},
                {0x01, "Sleep until resume is detected"}
            }
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 3) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 5));
        PARAM "Resume_Timeout"
            uint8
            doc "Applicable To Sleep Modes 3, 5."
                "After the device issues a USB RESUME, it will wait this many seconds for the"
                "Host to resume USB operations before issuing another USB RESUME."
                "If this value is set to 0, the device will never reissue RESUME"
                "and will instead wait forever for the host to act on the initial RESUME."
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 3) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 5));
        PARAM "Enable_BREAK_To_Host"
            bool8
            doc "Applicable To Sleep Mode 12."
                "If 0 and sleep mode 12 is selected, disables setting a break"
                "condition to the host, making the sleep mechanism unidirectional."
            default = 1
            enabled_if ("Sleep_Mode" = 12);
        PARAM "Pulsed_HOST_WAKE"
            bool8
            doc "Applicable To Sleep Modes 1, 12."
                "After asserting BT_WAKE (mode 1) or setting or clearing a BREAK condition (mode "
                "12), if the host does not wake up, clear the condition and retry."
            enabled_if      (("Sleep_Mode" = 1)
                        or  (("Sleep_Mode" = 12) and ("Enable_BREAK_To_Host" = 1)));
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
    }

    COMMAND "Read_Sleepmode_Param" 0x028
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0

                        ("Sleep_Mode" = 5) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 7));

            bool8
            doc "See Set_Sleepmode_Param."
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 1) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 2) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 7));
        CC_PARAM "Active_Connection_Handling_On_Suspend"
            uint8
            doc "See Set_Sleepmode_Param."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "Maintain connections; sleep when timed activity allows"},
                {0x01, "Sleep until resume is detected"}
            }
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 3) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 5));
        CC_PARAM "Resume_Timeout"
            uint8
            doc "See Set_Sleepmode_Param."
            enabled_if (("Sleep_Mode" = 3) or ("Sleep_Mode" = 5));
        CC_PARAM "Enable_BREAK_To_Host"

            enabled_if ("Sleep_Mode" = 12);
        CC_PARAM "Pulsed_HOST_WAKE"
            bool8
            doc "See Set_Sleepmode_Param."
            enabled_if      (("Sleep_Mode" = 1)
                        or  (("Sleep_Mode" = 12) and ("Enable_BREAK_To_Host" = 1)));
