    COMMAND "FM_TX_Command" 0x082
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2048"=1
        doc "This command encapsulates a variety of FM_RDS I2C bus transactions."
        PARAM "I2C_Address"
            uint8
            doc "The register to be addressed."
            enum
            {
                                {0xd2, "I2C_FMTX_RDS_DEV_SET"},
                                {0xd3, "I2C_FMTX_PILOT_DEV_SET"},
                                {0xd4, "I2C_FMTX_AUDIO_DEV_SET"},
                                {0xa2, "I2C_FMTX_REF_ERR_SET"},
                {0x63, "I2C_FMTX_MUTE"},
                {0x6b, "I2C_FMTX_CHANL_SET"},
                {0x6d, "I2C_FMTX_CHANL_BW_SET"},
                {0x70, "I2C_FMTX_REF_SET"},
                {0x71, "I2C_FMTX_LOCK_GET"},
                {0x72, "I2C_FMTX_PA_CONTROL_SET"},
                                {0x51, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_AFC_LC"},
                                {0x4F, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_AFC_RC"},
                                {0x53, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_FC_LC"},
                                {0x55, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_FC_RC"},
                {0x74, "I2C_FMTX_DEV_SET"},
                                {0x75, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_SET"},
                                {0x57, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_MRK_A"},
                                {0x59, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_MRK_B"},
                                {0x5B, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_SPC_A"},
                                {0x5D, "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_SPC_B"},
                                {0x7B, "I2C_FMTX_AUDIO_LOW_COUNT"},
                                {0x7C, "I2C_FMTX_AUDIO_HIGH_COUNT"},
                                {0x7D, "I2C_FMTX_AUDIO_THRESH_LOW"},
                                {0x7E, "I2C_FMTX_AUDIO_THRESH_HIGH"},
                {0xd7, "I2C_FMTX_AUDIO_DETECT_STATUS"},
                {0x80, "I2C_FMTX_RDS_DATA_SET"},
                {0x97, "I2C_FMTX_MPX_LMT_SET"},
                {0xa3, "I2C_FMTX_CTRL_SET"},
                {0xa4, "I2C_FMTX_PI_SET"},
                {0xa6, "I2C_FMTX_TYPE_SET"},
                {0xa8, "I2C_FMTX_AF"},
                {0xa9, "I2C_FMTX_DISPLAY_SIZE"},
                {0xaa, "I2C_FMTX_RDS_MODE"},
                {0xab, "I2C_PAGE_CONTEXT"},
                {0xad, "I2C_FMTX_SCROLL_RATE"},
                {0xaf, "I2C_FMTX_TOGGLE_AB"},
                {0xb0, "I2C_FMTX_RDS_REP_SET"},
                {0xb1, "I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET"},
                {0xbb, "I2C_FMTX_IRQ_MASK"},
                {0xbf, "I2C_FMTX_IRQ_FLAG"},
                {0xc9, "I2C_FMTX_RDS_WLINE"},
                                {0xd0, "I2C_FMTX_RANGE"},
                {0x77, "I2C_FMTX_SCAN_START"},
                {0x79, "I2C_FMTX_SCAN_END"},
                {0xF1, "I2C_FMTX_VARICAP_VAL"},
                {0xe0, "I2C_FMTX_BURST_MODE"},
                {0xfb, "I2C_FM_AUDIO_LEVEL_RESULT"}
            };
        PARAM "Read_Write_Mode"
            uint8
            doc "Whether the register is being read or written."
            enum
            {
                {0, "Write"},
                {1, "Read"}
            };
        PARAM "Read_Length"
            uint8
            doc "If Read_Write_Mode is 1 (Read), this parameter is included and indicates the"
                "length in bytes of data to be read, else it is excluded. This parameter is"
                "explicitly included for use in translation to an I2C bus transaction, but is fixed"
                "for a given I2C bus address."
            present_if ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1)
            binary_message_only
            encode_value =  if      ("Read_Byte_Count_Override" = 1) then "Read_Byte_Count"
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x4f) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x51) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x53) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x55) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x57) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x59) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x5B) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x5D) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x63) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x6b) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x6d) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x70) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x71) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x72) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x74) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x75) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x77) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x79) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x7b) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x7c) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x7d) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x7e) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x97) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xa2) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xa3) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xa4) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xa6) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xa8) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xa9) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xaa) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xab) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xad) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xaf) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xb0) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xb1) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xbb) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xbf) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xc9) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xd0) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xd2) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xd3) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xd7) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xe0) then 2
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xd4) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xf1) then 1
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xfb) then 4
                            else                                255;    # Error
        PARAM "Read_Byte_Count_Override"
            bool8
            doc "An abstraction by which to override the byte count to read from any"
                "register."
            not_in_binary_message
            enabled_if ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1)
            decode_value =  if  ("I2C_Address" = 0x63)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x4f)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x51)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x53)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0

                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0

                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x5D)
                                then if ( ("Read_Length" < 2) or ("Read_Length" > 2) ) then 1 else 0

                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x6d)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x70)

                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0x79)

                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0

                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xa8)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xa9)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xaa)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0

                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0

                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xd7)
                                then if ("Read_Length" > 1) then 1 else 0
                            else if ("I2C_Address" = 0xfb)
                                then if (("Read_Length" > 4) or ("Read_Length" < 4) ) then 4 else 0
                            else                                1;  # Error;

        PARAM "Read_Byte_Count"
            uint8
            doc "An abstraction by which to provide the byte count to read from registers if Read_Byte_Count_Override is enabled."
            not_in_binary_message
            enabled_if (    ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1)
                            and ("Read_Byte_Count_Override" = 1)  )
            decode_value = "Read_Length"
            max = 250;

        ############################################################################################
        # Write access -I2C_FMTX_RDS_DATA_SET
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_DATA_SET"
            uint8[252] omit_pad_bytes
            doc "In automatic mode, ASCII characters for the intended display text. "
                " In manual mode, 3 2 bytes per block representing block byte 0, block byte 1, then the block typethe two block data bytes.  Given that each RDS group is comprised of four blocks (A, B, C or C', D), in manual mode the data must be written in multiples of eight bytes and must represent one or more complete RDS groups.  If this restriction is not adhered to, a hardware error event will be generated over the HCI transport.  "
                " NOTE: pending Nokia's approval.  They call for firmware to embed the block type, but doing so would prevent them from loading the FIFO with a long message train then going to sleep they would have to be actively involved in committing one block at a time and waiting for the  COMMIT_COMPLETE interrupt for each block, impacting their power consumption.  With our 510 byte preFIFO, the one byte per block cost is preferable from every perspective. "
                " The data is not retrieved until the host writes to I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET."

            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x80) )
            valid_length = ByteArrayValidLength("I2C_FMTX_RDS_DATA_SET")
                min = 1;
        ############################################################################################

        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_SCROLL_STEP"
            uint8{7:2}
            doc "fmtx_rds_disp_mode: Not applicable in manual mode.  Tells the FMTX block to scroll the characters in PS mode.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written."
                "fmtx_rds_disp_modescroll_step: 0=disable scroll, 1 to 15=enable scroll, sets the number of characters to scroll at a time."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xaa) )
            max = 15;
        PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_MODE"
            uint8{1:0}
            doc "fmtx_rds_mode: RDS TX master enable/disable.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written.  When enabled, sets the FMTX block into automatic mode where it constructs RDS groups automatically, or manual mode where the FMTX block interprets the character set as direct block data."
           "When the mode is set to manual repeating and I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written, the entire FIFO contents are copied to a temporary working buffer by firmware, and that entire block train will be sent repeatedly until commanded to do otherwise."
            "When new data is sent to the device for transmission, regardless of whether the mode is automatic or manual, the group currently being transmitted or sequence of groups will be sent to completion depending on the commit type (COMMIT_NOW or COMMIT_DEFERRED), with the new RDS message sequence taking effect at that time.  When the mode is changed from either on mode to off, all committed RDS information cached by firmware will be flushed."
            "Thus, no more RDS blocks will be sent until new RDS data is committed by this register has been used to reenable RDS and I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET having been issued.  When the mode is set to off, the RDS FIFOs (preFIFO, rdsFIFO) are flushed.  When in any mode other than off, the only valid mode transition is to the off state.  Direct transitions between the various on modes will result in undefined behavior."
               "fmtx_rds_mode: 0=off, 1=automatic, 2=manual, 3=manual repeating."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xaa) );
        ############################################################################################
        # Write access -I2C_PAGE_CONTEXT
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_PAGE_CONTEXT"
            uint8
            doc "Selects between RX and TX mode.  Any time the page is switched, any pending FM interrupts (I2C_FM&RDS_FLAG, I2C_FMTX_IRQ_FLAG) from device to host will be cleared by device firmware. 0 = RX, 1= TX"
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xab) )
            max = 1;
        ############################################################################################
        # Write access -I2C_FMTX_SCROLL_RATE
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FMTX_SCROLL_RATE"
            uint8
            doc "Not applicable in manual mode.  Sets the rate at which the PS display characters are scrolled.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written."
                "Meaning TBD Delay between two consecutive display scrolls, from 0 to 7.5 seconds in units of 500 ms."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xad) );
        ############################################################################################
        # Write access -I2C_FMTX_TOGGLE_AB
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FMTX_TOGGLE_AB"
            uint8
            doc "Not applicable in manual mode.  Indicates a new message.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written.  If not toggled then the receiver assumes same message is being transmitted and over writes message. This can mean that in poor reception a complete message could be received."
                "When set to 1 the A/B bit in Block B of type 2 groups is toggled from its current value to clear the RDS RT display. Setting this bit should cause the FMTX block to send all future type 2 groups with the A/B bit inverted from its previous state.  The register Bit is cleared automatically."
                "When written with a 1, triggers an A/B inversion."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xaf) )
            max = 1;
        ############################################################################################
        # Write access -I2C_FMTX_RDS_REP_SET
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_REP_SEL"
            uint8
            doc "Not applicable in manual mode.  Sets the repertoire in use. The FMTX block should prefix the RT or PS message sent with two control characters corresponding to the repertoire selected.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written."
                " 0=G0 (0F,0F), 1=G1 (0E,0E), 2=G2 (1B,6E)."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xb0) )
            max = 2;
        ############################################################################################
        # Write access -I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET
        ############################################################################################
        PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT"
            uint8
            doc "Indicates to the FM device that a new set of RDS parameters have been loaded and are ready to be used for constructing the next RDS message. Also indicates to the FM device when to stop the RDS data stream."
                "Writing to this register causes an interrupt to the firmware. Exactly one bit must be set per write access; therefore, valid write data words are 1, 2, 4, and 8. The COMMIT_NOW or COMMIT_DEFERRED bit should be set whenever a new set of RDS parameters (PI, TYPE, PTY, A/B, etc) have been loaded for the next message."
               "COMMIT_NOW causes the new settings to take effect immediately, i.e. on the next outgoing RDS group COMMIT_DEFFERRED causes the new parameters to take effect after the current RDS message sequence has been transmitted. STOP_NOW causes the RDS data stream to stop as soon as the HW has finished transmitting the current RDS group. STOP_DEFERRED causes the RDS data stream to stop after the HW has finished transmitting the current RDS message."
               "An interrupt is generated towards the host by the FM device after it has received an I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET command and finished loading the new RDS parameters.  The host must not write new RDS parameters between the time it sent the COMMIT command and the time it received a COMMIT_COMPLETE back from the FM device. This prevents race conditions.  See I2C_FMTX_IRQ_FLAG1."
               " The following values are collected when COMMIT_SET is received and the mode is not manual or manual repeating: I2C_FMTX_PI_SET0, I2C_FMTX_PI_SET1, I2C_FMTX_TYPE_SET, I2C_FMTX_AF, I2C_FMTX_DISPLAY_SIZE, I2C_FMTX_SCROLL_RATE, I2C_FMTX_TOGGLE_AB, I2C_FMTX_RDS_REP_SET."
               "The following data is collected when COMMIT_SET is received: I2C_FMTX_RDS_MODE, RDS FIFO contents."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xb1) )
            bitmap
            {
                {0x0001, "COMMIT_NOW"},
                {0x0002, "COMMIT_DEFFERED"}
            };
        ############################################################################################

        ############################################################################################

            doc "FMTX RANGE"
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xd0) )
            max = 3;

        PARAM "I2C_FMTX_IRQ_MASK"
            uint16
            doc "Interrupt mask register for the FM interrupts, masking the contents of the I2C_FMTX_IRQ_FLAG register. The FM IRQ mask and flag registers are auto cleared when the two 8-bit flag registers are read in succession in this order: I2C_FMTX_IRQ_FLAG0 and I2C_FMTX_IRQ_FLAG1; reading in any other order, or reads with intervening bus activity, produce undefined results."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 0) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xbb) )
            bitmap
            {
                {0x0001, "SOFT_CLIP_DET"},

                                {0x0010, "AUDIO_STATUS_CHANGED"},

                {0x0100, "COMMIT_COMPLETE"},

            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x4F) )
            max = 0x7FFF;

        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "I2C_FMTX_CHRP_FC_RC"

            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x55) )
            max = 0xFFF;
        ############################################################################################
        # Read access -I2C_FMTX_RDS_DATA_SET
        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_DATA_SET"
            uint8[252] omit_pad_bytes
            doc "In automatic mode, ASCII characters for the intended display text. "
                " In manual mode, 3 2 bytes per block representing block byte 0, block byte 1, then the block typethe two block data bytes.  Given that each RDS group is comprised of four blocks (A, B, C or C', D), in manual mode the data must be written in multiples of eight bytes and must represent one or more complete RDS groups.  If this restriction is not adhered to, a hardware error event will be generated over the HCI transport.  "
                " NOTE: pending Nokia's approval.  They call for firmware to embed the block type, but doing so would prevent them from loading the FIFO with a long message train then going to sleep they would have to be actively involved in committing one block at a time and waiting for the  COMMIT_COMPLETE interrupt for each block, impacting their power consumption.  With our 510 byte preFIFO, the one byte per block cost is preferable from every perspective. "
                " The data is not retrieved until the host writes to I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET."

            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0x80) );
        ############################################################################################

        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_SCROLL_STEP"
            uint8{7:2}
            doc "fmtx_rds_disp_mode: Not applicable in manual mode.  Tells the FMTX block to scroll the characters in PS mode.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written."
                "fmtx_rds_disp_modescroll_step: 0=disable scroll, 1 to 15=enable scroll, sets the number of characters to scroll at a time."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xaa) )
            max = 15;
        CC_PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_MODE"
            uint8{1:0}
            doc "fmtx_rds_mode: RDS TX master enable/disable.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written.  When enabled, sets the FMTX block into automatic mode where it constructs RDS groups automatically, or manual mode where the FMTX block interprets the character set as direct block data."
           "When the mode is set to manual repeating and I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written, the entire FIFO contents are copied to a temporary working buffer by firmware, and that entire block train will be sent repeatedly until commanded to do otherwise."
            "When new data is sent to the device for transmission, regardless of whether the mode is automatic or manual, the group currently being transmitted or sequence of groups will be sent to completion depending on the commit type (COMMIT_NOW or COMMIT_DEFERRED), with the new RDS message sequence taking effect at that time.  When the mode is changed from either on mode to off, all committed RDS information cached by firmware will be flushed."
            "Thus, no more RDS blocks will be sent until new RDS data is committed by this register has been used to reenable RDS and I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET having been issued.  When the mode is set to off, the RDS FIFOs (preFIFO, rdsFIFO) are flushed.  When in any mode other than off, the only valid mode transition is to the off state.  Direct transitions between the various on modes will result in undefined behavior."
               "fmtx_rds_mode: 0=off, 1=automatic, 2=manual, 3=manual repeating."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xaa) );

            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xab) )

        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "I2C_FMTX_TOGGLE_AB"
            uint8
            doc "Not applicable in manual mode.  Indicates a new message.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written.  If not toggled then the receiver assumes same message is being transmitted and over writes message. This can mean that in poor reception a complete message could be received."
                "When set to 1 the A/B bit in Block B of type 2 groups is toggled from its current value to clear the RDS RT display. Setting this bit should cause the FMTX block to send all future type 2 groups with the A/B bit inverted from its previous state.  The register Bit is cleared automatically."
                "When written with a 1, triggers an A/B inversion."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xaf) )
            max = 1;
        ############################################################################################
        # Read access -I2C_FMTX_RDS_REP_SET
        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_REP_SEL"
            uint8
            doc "Not applicable in manual mode.  Sets the repertoire in use. The FMTX block should prefix the RT or PS message sent with two control characters corresponding to the repertoire selected.  It does not take effect until I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET is written."
                " 0=G0 (0F,0F), 1=G1 (0E,0E), 2=G2 (1B,6E)."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xb0) )
            max = 2;
        ############################################################################################
        # Read access -I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET
        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT"
            uint8
            doc "Indicates to the FM device that a new set of RDS parameters have been loaded and are ready to be used for constructing the next RDS message. Also indicates to the FM device when to stop the RDS data stream."
                "Writing to this register causes an interrupt to the firmware. Exactly one bit must be set per write access; therefore, valid write data words are 1, 2, 4, and 8. The COMMIT_NOW or COMMIT_DEFERRED bit should be set whenever a new set of RDS parameters (PI, TYPE, PTY, A/B, etc) have been loaded for the next message."
               "COMMIT_NOW causes the new settings to take effect immediately, i.e. on the next outgoing RDS group COMMIT_DEFFERRED causes the new parameters to take effect after the current RDS message sequence has been transmitted. STOP_NOW causes the RDS data stream to stop as soon as the HW has finished transmitting the current RDS group. STOP_DEFERRED causes the RDS data stream to stop after the HW has finished transmitting the current RDS message."
               "An interrupt is generated towards the host by the FM device after it has received an I2C_FMTX_RDS_COMMIT_SET command and finished loading the new RDS parameters.  The host must not write new RDS parameters between the time it sent the COMMIT command and the time it received a COMMIT_COMPLETE back from the FM device. This prevents race conditions.  See I2C_FMTX_IRQ_FLAG1."
               " The following values are collected when COMMIT_SET is received and the mode is not manual or manual repeating: I2C_FMTX_PI_SET0, I2C_FMTX_PI_SET1, I2C_FMTX_TYPE_SET, I2C_FMTX_AF, I2C_FMTX_DISPLAY_SIZE, I2C_FMTX_SCROLL_RATE, I2C_FMTX_TOGGLE_AB, I2C_FMTX_RDS_REP_SET."
               "The following data is collected when COMMIT_SET is received: I2C_FMTX_RDS_MODE, RDS FIFO contents."
            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xb1) )
            bitmap
            {
                {0x0001, "COMMIT_NOW"},
                {0x0002, "COMMIT_DEFFERED"}
            };

        ############################################################################################

        ############################################################################################

            present_if ( ("Read_Write_Mode" = 1) and ("I2C_Address" = 0xbb) )

    COMMAND "Write_Dynamic_SCO_Routing_Change" 0x083
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "This command will write dynamic SCO routing enable."

        PARAM "SCO_Routing"
            uint8
            doc "Specifies whether the SCO path is through PCM interface or transport"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "PCM"},
                {0x01, "Transport"},
                {0x02, "CODEC"},
                {0x03, "I2S"},
                {0xFF, "No Change"}

            }
            default = 0;

        PARAM "SCO_Connections"
            uint8
            doc "Specifies the SCO connection number"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "0"},
                {0x01, "1"},
                {0x02, "2"},
                {0x03, "3"}

            };


        PARAM "Connection_Handle[0]"
            connhandle16
            doc "Specifies the SCO connection handle"
            present_if ( "SCO_Connections" > 0 );
        PARAM "SCO_Routing[0]"
            uint8
            doc "Specifies whether the SCO path is through PCM interface or transport"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "PCM"},
                {0x01, "Transport"},
                {0x02, "CODEC"},
                {0x03, "I2S"}
            }
            default = 0
            present_if ( "SCO_Connections" > 0 );

        PARAM "PCM_Time_Slot[0]"
            uint16

                {0x0, "Linear"},

    COMMAND "Read_HID_Bit_Error_Rate" 0x084

    COMMAND "Enable_HCI_Remote_Test" 0x085
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2035"=1
        doc "This command will write the new connection timeout value."
        PARAM "Config"
            uint8
            doc "Specifies the amount of time that the device will send IDs after"
                "receiving an ID in response to an FHS packet that was sent during"
                "master response. Also specifies the amount of time that a device"
                "will scan for a POLL packet after sending an ID in response to"
                "an FHS packet that was received during slave response."
            bitmap
            {
                {0x01, "DUT device"},
                {0x02, "Remote device"},
                {0x04, "Filter local device events"},
                {0x08, "Filter remote device events"}
            };

        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
    }

    # COMMAND "Reserved, customer-specific, confidential" 0x086

    COMMAND "Calibrate_Bandgap" 0x08a
        {
                devs "*"=0, "BCM20730"=1, "BCM20733"=1
                doc     "This command calibrates ADC Bandgap value. Before calling this command,"
                        "the Bandgap calibration virtual channel must be determined and an accurate"
                        "voltage must be applied. When called, the device will read channel"
                        "value and calibrate the Bandgap value based on the input voltage."
                PARAM "Voltage"
                        uint16
                        doc     "The accurate voltage value in microvolts that applies to the calibrating"
                                "virtual channel."
                        note "microvolts";
                PARAM "ADC_Channel"
                        uint8
                        doc "The ADC channel"

            enum
            {
               {0, "ADC_INPUT_VSS"},

               {12, "ADC_INPUT_P23"},

    COMMAND "UIPC_Over_HCI" 0x08B
    {
        devs "*"=1

        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;

        #
        #  UIPC header
        #
        CC_PARAM "Event"
            uint16
            doc "UIPC event"
            enum
            {
                {0x9000, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_LOGMSG_EVT"},
                {0x9001, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_ACL_EVT"},
                {0x9002, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_BTU_EVT"},
                {0x9003, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_L2C_EVT"},
                {0x9004, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_L2C_MSG_EVT"},
                {0x9005, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_BTM_EVT"},
                {0x9006, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_AVDT_EVT"},
                {0x9007, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_SLIP_EVT"},
                {0x9008, "BT_EVT_BTU_IPC_MGMT_EVT"}
            }
            present_if ("Status" = 0);
        #
        #  UIPC
        #
        CC_PARAM "IPC Opcode"
            uint8
            doc "UIPC Opcode"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "UIPC_OPEN_REQ"},
                {0x01, "UIPC_OPEN_RESP"},
                {0x02, "UIPC_CLOSE_REQ"},
                {0x03, "UIPC_CLOSE_RESP"}
            }
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9008));
        CC_PARAM "Open Status"
            uint8
            doc "Open Status"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9008) and ("IPC Opcode" = 0x01));
        CC_PARAM "Major Version"
            uint16
            doc "Major Version"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9008) and ("IPC Opcode" = 0x01));
        CC_PARAM "Minor Version"
            uint16
            doc "Minor Version"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9008) and ("IPC Opcode" = 0x01));
        CC_PARAM "NumStreams"
            uint8
            doc "NumStreams"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9008) and ("IPC Opcode" = 0x01));
        #
        #  L2CAP
        #
        CC_PARAM "L2C Opcode"
            uint8
            doc "L2C Opcode"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "L2C_SYNC_TO_LITE_REQ"},
                {0x01, "L2C_SYNC_TO_LITE_RESP"},
                {0x02, "L2C_REMOVE_TO_LITE_REQ"},
                {0x03, "L2C_REMOVE_TO_LITE_RESP"},
                {0x04, "L2C_FLUSH_TO_FULL_IND"}
            }
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9003));
        CC_PARAM "L2C Num Unacked"
            uint16
            doc "Num Unacked"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9003)
                            and (("L2C Opcode" = 0x01) or ("L2C Opcode" = 0x03)));
        CC_PARAM "L2C Num Stream"
            uint8
            doc "Num Stream"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9003)
                        and (("L2C Opcode" = 0x01) or ("L2C Opcode" = 0x03)));
        CC_PARAM "LCID[0]"
            uint16
            doc "LCID"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9003)
                        and (("L2C Opcode" = 0x01) or ("L2C Opcode" = 0x03))
                        and ("L2C Num Stream" > 0));
        CC_PARAM "status[0]"
            uint8
            doc "status"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "L2C_SYNC_SUCCESS"},
                {0x01, "L2C_SYNC_FAILURE"}
            }
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9003)
                        and (("L2C Opcode" = 0x01) or ("L2C Opcode" = 0x03))
                        and ("L2C Num Stream" > 0));
        CC_PARAM "LCID[1]"
            uint16
            doc "LCID"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9003)
                        and (("L2C Opcode" = 0x01) or ("L2C Opcode" = 0x03))
                        and ("L2C Num Stream" > 1));
        CC_PARAM "status[1]"
            uint8
            doc "status"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "L2C_SYNC_SUCCESS"},
                {0x01, "L2C_SYNC_FAILURE"}
            }
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9003)
                        and (("L2C Opcode" = 0x01) or ("L2C Opcode" = 0x03))
                        and ("L2C Num Stream" > 1));
        #
        #  AVDT Sync message
        #
        CC_PARAM "AVDT Opcode"
            uint8
            doc "Opcode"
            enum
            {
                {0x08, "AVDT_SYNC_TO_BTC_LITE_REQ"},
                {0x09, "AVDT_SYNC_TO_BTC_LITE_RESP"}
            }
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9006));
        CC_PARAM "AVDT status"
            uint8
            doc "status"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "AVDT_SYNC_SUCCESS"},
                {0x01, "AVDT_SYNC_FAILURE"}
            }
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9006));
        #
        #  BTA AV Sync message
        #
        CC_PARAM "BTA AV Opcode"
            uint8
            doc "Opcode"
            enum
            {
                {0x0b, "A2DP_START_REQ"},
                {0x0c, "A2DP_START_RESP"},
                {0x0d, "A2DP_STOP_REQ"},
                {0x0e, "A2DP_STOP_RESP"},
                {0x0f, "A2DP_CLEANUP_REQ"},
                {0x10, "A2DP_CLEANUP_RESP"},
                {0x11, "A2DP_SUSPEND_REQ"},
                {0x12, "A2DP_SUSPEND_RESP"},
                {0x13, "AUDIO_CODEC_CONFIG_REQ"},
                {0x14, "AUDIO_CODEC_CONFIG_RESP"},
                {0x15, "AUDIO_CODEC_SET_BITRATE_REQ"},
                {0x16, "AUDIO_CODEC_FLUSH_REQ"},
                {0x17, "AUDIO_ROUTE_CONFIG_REQ"},
                {0x18, "AUDIO_ROUTE_CONFIG_RESP"},
                {0x19, "AUDIO_MIX_CONFIG_REQ"},
                {0x1a, "AUDIO_MIX_CONFIG_RESP"},
                {0x1b, "AUDIO_BURST_FRAMES_IND"}
            }
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9005));
        CC_PARAM "LCID"
            uint16
            doc "LCID"
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9005)
                    and (("BTA AV Opcode" = 0x0c) or ("BTA AV Opcode" = 0x0e)
                     or ("BTA AV Opcode" = 0x10) or ("BTA AV Opcode" = 0x12)));
        CC_PARAM "Config Status"
            uint8
            doc "config status"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "AUDIO_CONFIG_SUCCESS"},
                {0x01, "AUDIO_CONFIG_NOT_SUPPORTED"},
                {0x02, "AUDIO_CONFIG_FAIL_OUT_OF_MEMORY"},
                {0x03, "AUDIO_CONFIG_FAIL_CODEC_USED"},
                {0x04, "AUDIO_CONFIG_FAIL_ROUTE"}
            }
            present_if (("Status" = 0) and ("Event" = 0x9005)
                        and (("BTA AV Opcode" = 0x14) or ("BTA AV Opcode" = 0x18)
                             or ("BTA AV Opcode" = 0x1b)));
    }

   COMMAND "GPS_Sensor_Inquire" 0x8f
   {
      devs "*"=0, "BCM2075"=1
      doc "VSC for sensor commands"

      PARAM "Command"
         uint8
         doc "Command to the sensor driver. "
         enum
         {
            {0x0, "Open"},
            {0x1, "Close"},
            {0x2, "Reset"},
            {0x3, "Generic I2C msg"},
            {0x4, "Read sensor data"},
            {0x6, "Set sensor mode"},
            {0x7, "Write sensor configuration"},
            {0x8, "Read sensor configuration"}
         };

      PARAM "Sensor ID"
         uint16
         doc "Sensor ID. Lower 10-bit is sensor address. "
             "If address type is 10-bit, set bit 15 to 1."
             "For a 10-bit address, if true 10-bit addressing"
             "is used, i.e.,2-byte format is used, set bit 14 to 1"
             "If only 1-byte is used for address, 2nd byte is"
             "part of data, set bit 14 to 0"
         enum
         {
            {0x1F, "AK8973 (Broadcom)"},
            {0x0F, "KXTE9 (Broadcom)"}
         };

      PARAM "Response Type"
         uint8
         doc "Response type expected"
         enum
         {
            {0x0, "No Response Required"},
            {0x1, "Immediate Response Required"},
            {0x2, "Immediate plus refresh"},
            {0x3, "Following execution"}
         };

      PARAM "Data Size"
         uint8
         doc "Bytes in payload data"
         binary_message_only
         encode_value = ByteArrayValidLength("Data")
         min = 0x00
         max = 0xF0;

      PARAM "Data"
         uint8[251] omit_pad_bytes
         doc "The written bytes."
         valid_length = "Data Size";
    }

    COMMAND "Write_RF_Reprogramming_Table_Masking" 0x0A0
        {
        devs    "*"=1,
                "BCM2035"=1 ("Flash based systems or ROM with patch only."),
                "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "This command allows the user to modify selected bits in an RF register"
            "This command modifies default RF register settings. This command is only supported by"
            "flash based systems or systems with the required patch installed."
        PARAM "Number_Of_Entries"
            uint8
            doc "Number of settings to modify."
            min = 1
            max = 10;

        # - Entry[0] - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
        PARAM "Register_Offset[0]"
            uint8
            doc "RF Register offset for the associated entry. This parameter will be repeated for"
                "Number_Of_Entries, with all array parameters interleaved according to Bluetooth"
                "standard array parameter treatment.";
        PARAM "Data_Mask[0]"
            uint8
            doc "Data mask which selects which bits in the register will be modified by the entry"
                "For example, if the mask is 0x18 only bits 3 and 4 will be modified";
        PARAM "Register_Value[0]"
            uint8
            doc "RF Register value for the associated entry. This parameter will be repeated for"
                "Number_Of_Entries, with all array parameters interleaved according to Bluetooth"
                "standard array parameter treatment.";

        # - Entry[1] - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
        PARAM "Register_Offset[1]"
            uint8
            present_if ("Number_Of_Entries" > 1);

                "For example, if the mask is 0x18 only bits 3 and 4 will be modified"

            uint8
            doc "Action"
            enum
            {
                {0, "Read status of calibration, return calibration factors."},

                {2, "Start test. Return results. Store results to SS if Pass."},
                {3, "Stop test. Return results. Do not store results to SS"},
                {4, "Read calibration factors stored in SS."},
                {5, "Store input calibration factors to SS."}
            };

        PARAM "Test duration"
            uint16

            "Mic calibration factor for Mic1 if the action is to save calib to SS."

            max = 65535

            note "num 5ms frames";

            max = 32767

            int16
            doc "Calibration factor for gaindB if the action is to save calib to SS."
            min = -32768
            max = 32767
            default = 0
            note "dB (Q8 format)";

        ############################################################################################
        # Command Complete event
        ############################################################################################
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;

        CC_PARAM "Result_status"
            uint8
            doc "The status of the command executed"

            {
                {0, "PASS"},
                {1, "FAIL"},
                {2, "INCOMPLETE"},
                {3, "SS_WRITE_FAIL"}
            };


        CC_PARAM "Mic1 Correction Factor"
            int16
            doc "The correctionfactor for Mic1 in dB in Q8 format";

        CC_PARAM "Mic2 Correction Factor"
            int16
            doc "The correctionfactor for Mic2 in dB in Q8 format";

            enabled_if(("HW_Config" & 0x200) = 0x200);

            connhandle16
            doc "Connection Handle"

            enabled_if( (("HW_Config" & 0x200) = 0x200) or (("HW_Config" & 0x400) = 0x400) );

            uint16
            doc "event count offset"
            default = 10;

            uint8
            doc "Window size used for the anchor point of a connection event"

            max = 16

            default = 0x65;

                {0x4, "master SCA 51 ppm to 75 ppm"},
                {0x5, "master SCA 31 ppm to 50 ppm"},
                {0x6, "master SCA 21 ppm to 30 ppm"},
                {0x7, "master SCA 0 ppm to 20 ppm"}
            }
            enabled_if(("HW_Config" & 0x1000) = 0x1000);
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8
            doc "Return status. 0x00=BT_SUCCESS, 0x01=Command Not Supported";
    }

        CC_PARAM "TxRxPair"
            uint8
            doc "Current setting: Tx/Rx Pairs for each connection event"
            present_if ("Action" = 1);
        CC_PARAM "Repeat_Count"
            uint32
            doc "Current setting: Number of packets to be sent"
            present_if ("Action" = 1);
        CC_PARAM "ConnectionInterval"
            uint16
            doc "Current setting: Connection Interval. Unit: frames(1.25ms)"
            present_if ("Action" = 1);
        CC_PARAM "AcceptBadCrc"
            bool8
            doc "Current setting: Option to send TX when a RX packet has bad CRC"
            present_if ("Action" = 1);
        CC_PARAM "PacketType"
            uint8
            doc "Current setting: Packet type to be used in the packet header"
            present_if ("Action" = 1);
        CC_PARAM "Payload_Length"
            uint8
            doc "Current setting: Number of bytes in the payload"
            present_if ("Action" = 1);
        CC_PARAM "Payload"
            uint8[37] omit_pad_bytes
            doc "Current setting: Payload data"
            valid_length = "Payload_Length"
            min = 0
            present_if (("Action" = 1) and ("Payload_Length">0));
    }

    COMMAND "LE_DVT_ErrorInject_Scheme" 0x0BF
    {
        devs "*"=0, "BCM2049"=1, "BCM2076"=1, "BCM20702"=1, "BCM20730"=1, "BCM4330"=1
        doc "This command is for use for testing to inject various errors in different scenarios"
        PARAM "Action"
            uint8
            doc "WRITE: sets error scheme. READ: reads the current error scheme for the specific module"
            enum
            {
                {0, "Write"},
                {1, "Read"}
            };
        PARAM "ModuleId"
            uint8
            doc "The module that the error scheme is for"
            enum
            {
                {0, "ConnTest"},
                {1, "Scan"},
                {2, "Adv"},
                {3, "Init"},
                {4, "Conn"},
                {5, "Test"}
            }
            default = 0;
        PARAM "ErrorType"
            uint8
            doc "Type of the error"
            present_if ("Action" = 0)
            enum
            {
                {0, "NONE"},
                {1, "CrcError"},
                {2, "NoTx"},
                {3, "NoRx"},
                {4, "BadAccAddr"},
                {5, "HeaderError"},
                {6, "PostMICHeaderError"},
                {7, "MIC_Error"},
                {8, "SendDataPktAfterEncPause"},
                {9, "ConnUpdateTimeError"},
                {10,"NoAutoVersionId"},
                {11,"NoFeatureRsp"}
            }
            default = 0;
        PARAM "ScenarioType"
            uint8
            doc "Scenario to inject the error"
            present_if ("Action" = 0)
            enum
            {
                {1, "TxOnly"},
                {2, "RspOnly"},
                {3, "Tx_or_Rsp"},
                {4, "RxOnly"}
            }
            default = 3;
        PARAM "InitialGoodPacketStreamSize"
            uint16
            doc "Number of initial consecutive packets in which errors are not injected"
            present_if ("Action" = 0)
            min = 0
            max = 10000
            default = 0;
        PARAM "BadPacketStreamSize"
            uint16
            doc "Number of consecutive packets in which errors are injected after the initial packets or good packets below"
            present_if ("Action" = 0)
            min = 0
            max = 10000
            default = 0;
        PARAM "GoodPacketStreamSize"
            uint16
            doc "Number of consecutive packets in which errors are not injected after the bad packets above"
            present_if ("Action" = 0)
            min = 0
            max = 10000
            default = 0;
        PARAM "ErrorBitMask"
            uint32
            doc "Bit positions to inject certain errors. Used only for CrcError, BadAccAddr, HeaderError, PostMICHeaderError, MIC_Error"
            present_if ("Action" = 0)
            min = 0x00000000
            max = 0x00FFFFFF
            default = 0x00000000;
        PARAM "ConnTestPktFilter"
            uint32
            doc "Reserved for future use:  packet type filter to inject errors"
            bitmap
            {
                {0xFFFFFFFF, "AllPacketTypes"}
            }
            default = 0x00000000
            present_if ( ("Action" = 0) and ("ModuleId" = 0) );
        PARAM "ScanPktFilter"
            uint32
            doc "Reserved for future use:  packet type filter to inject errors"
            bitmap
            {
                {0xFFFFFFFF, "AllPacketTypes"}
            }
            default = 0x00000000
            present_if ( ("Action" = 0) and ("ModuleId" = 1) );
        PARAM "AdvPktFilter"
            uint32
            doc "Reserved for future use:  packet type filter to inject errors"
            bitmap
            {
                {0xFFFFFFFF, "AllPacketTypes"}
            }
            default = 0x00000000
            present_if ( ("Action" = 0) and ("ModuleId" = 2) );
        PARAM "InitPktFilter"
            uint32
            doc "Reserved for future use:  packet type filter to inject errors"

            {
                {0xFFFFFFFF, "AllPacketTypes"}
            }
            default = 0x00000000
            present_if ( ("Action" = 0) and ("ModuleId" = 3) );
        PARAM "ConnPktFilter_LLID"
            uint32{31:28}
            doc "Connection Packet LLID filter to inject errors"
            bitmap
            {
                {0x1, "0x0:Reserved"},
                {0x2, "0x1:LL DataPkt: Empty or L2CAP Cont."},
                {0x4, "0x2:LL DataPkt: Start or Complete L2CAP"},
                {0x8, "0x3:LL CtrlPkt"}
            }
            default=0
            present_if ( ("Action" = 0) and ("ModuleId" = 4) );
        PARAM "ConnPktFilterPktType"
            uint32{27:0}
            doc "Connection Packet type filter to inject errors"
            bitmap
            {
                {0x0000004, "(0x02)TERMINATE_IND"},
                {0x0000008, "(0x03)ENC_REQ"},
                {0x0000010, "(0x04)ENC_RSP"},
                {0x0000020, "(0x05)START_ENC_REQ"},
                {0x0000040, "(0x06)START_ENC_RSP"},
                {0x0000100, "(0x08)FEATURE_REQ"},
                {0x0000200, "(0x09)FEATURE_RSP"},
                {0x4000000, "DataPackets"},
                {0x8000000, "EmptyPackets"}
            }
            default = 0x00000000

    COMMAND "WBS_BEC_Params" 0x0D1
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "Specify the number of bits."
        PARAM "EnableTest"
            bool8
            doc "Enable test"
            default = 0;

            bool8
            doc "Specific bits to corrupt"
            default = 1
            present_if ("EnableTest" = 1 );

            bool8
            doc "Two State enable"
            default = 0
            present_if ("EnableTest" = 1 );

            uint32
            doc "Good State Jump probability"

            present_if (("EnableTest" = 1 ) and ("TwoStateEnableFlag" = 1 ) );
        PARAM "JMP_BAD_STATE"
            uint32
            doc "Bad State Jump probability"
            default = 0x6666666
            present_if (("EnableTest" = 1 ) and ("TwoStateEnableFlag" = 1 ) );
        PARAM "BAD_STATE_BER"
            uint32
            doc "Bad State BER"
            default = 0xF5C28F5

            present_if (("EnableTest" = 1 ) and ("TwoStateEnableFlag" = 0) and ("SpecificBitCorrputFlag" = 0));
        PARAM "BAD2_STATE_BER"

            default = 0xF5C28F5
            present_if (("EnableTest" = 1 ) and ("TwoStateEnableFlag" = 0) and ("SpecificBitCorrputFlag" = 0));

            uint16

            min = 0
            default = 1000
            present_if ("EnableTest" = 1 );

            max = 480

        PARAM "Packet1Bit_pos[1]"

            max = 480
            default = 57

    COMMAND "Super_Peek_Poke_with_BitMask" 0xD5
    {
        devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
        doc "Allows the RF registers and memory space to be read"
            "or written with bit mask. The parameters present are dependent on what memory space is being read or"
            "written and whether the access is a read or a write."
        PARAM "Access_Type"
            uint8
            doc "Selects which memory or register space type is to be read or written, and whether it"
                "is to be read or written."
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "BlueRF_Peek"},
                {0x01, "BlueRF_Poke"},
                {0x02, "ARM_Memory_Peek"},
                {0x03, "ARM_Memory_Poke"},
                {0x04, "PMU_Peek"},
                {0x05, "PMU_Poke"}
            };
        PARAM "BlueRF_Address"
            uint8
            doc "Present for RF register reads and writes. Specifies the RF register address to be"
                "read or written."
            present_if (("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x00));
        PARAM "ARM_Memory_Address"
            uint32
            doc "Present for memory reads and writes on ARM-based chips. Specifies the memory address"
                "to be read or written. For writes, the memory address must fall on a 32-bit"
                "address boundary."
            present_if (("Access_Type" = 0x02) or ("Access_Type" = 0x03));
        PARAM "PMU_Reg_Offset"
            uint8
            doc "Present for PMU register reads and writes. Specifies the PMU register address to be"
                "read or written."
            present_if (("Access_Type" = 0x05) or ("Access_Type" = 0x04));

        PARAM "BitMask8"
            uint8
            doc "Present for RF or PMU register writes on all chips."
                "Specifies the value to be written."
            present_if (    ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x05) );

        PARAM "Value8"
            uint8
            doc "Present for RF or PMU register writes on all chips."
                "Specifies the value to be written."
            present_if (    ("Access_Type" = 0x01) or ("Access_Type" = 0x05) );

        PARAM "BitMask32"
            uint32
            doc "Present for memory writes on ARM-based chips. Specifies the value to be written."
            present_if      ("Access_Type" = 0x03);

        PARAM "Value32"
            uint32
            doc "Present for memory writes on ARM-based chips. Specifies the value to be written."
            present_if      ("Access_Type" = 0x03);

        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;

        CC_PARAM "OriginalValue"
            uint32
            doc "The value read, which is only valid if the access type was a read.";

        CC_PARAM "NewValue"
            uint32
            doc "The value read, which is only valid if the access type was a read.";
    }

      COMMAND "Enable_H4IBSS" 0x0D7
      {
            devs "*"=1, "BCM2040"=0, "BCM2042"=0
            doc   "This command will activate the selected sleep mode algorithm"
                  "and specify respective timer thresholds."
            PARAM "Sleep_Mode"
                  uint8
                  doc   "Sleep mode algorithm selection."
                  enum
                  {
                        {0, "No sleep mode"},
                        {1, "N/A"},
                        {2, "N/A"},
                        {3, "N/A"},
                        {4, "H4IBSS"}
                  };
            PARAM "Idle_Threshold_Host"
                  uint8
                  doc   "Time interval of inactivity before the BT will allow the HT to enter "
                        "sleep mode."
                  enabled_if ("Sleep_Mode" = 4);

            PARAM "Idle_Threshold_HC"
                  uint8
                  doc   "Time interval of inactivity before the BT will consider entering "
                        "sleep mode."
                  enabled_if ("Sleep_Mode" = 4);

            PARAM "Sleep Guard Time"
                  uint8
                  doc   "Time interval before going to sleep after having sent SLEEP_ACK."
                  enabled_if ("Sleep_Mode" = 4);

            PARAM "Wake Guard Time"
                  uint8
                  doc   "Time interval to hold RTS low to signal a wakeup to peer device."
                  enabled_if ("Sleep_Mode" = 4);

            PARAM "Allow_Host_Sleep_During_SCO"
                  bool8
                  doc   "When this flag is set to 0, the host is not allowed to sleep while"
                        "an SCO is active. When this flag is set to 1, the host can sleep "
                        "while an SCO is active. This flag should only be set to 1 if SCO "
                        "traffic is directed to the PCM interface."
                  enabled_if ("Sleep_Mode" = 4);

            PARAM "TxDActiveMode"
                  uint8
                  doc   "This flag indicates whether the TxD line should be set high or low when the "
                        "controller goes to sleep."
                  enum
                  {
                        {0x00, "Low"},
                        {0x01, "High"}
                  }
                  enabled_if ("Sleep_Mode" = 4);

            CC_PARAM "Status"
                  errorcode8;
      }

    COMMAND "BlueBridge_SPI_Negotiation_Request" 0x0D8
    {
        devs "*"=1
        doc     "This command sends SPI Negotiation Request to the slave through Bluebridge master."
            "The slave will response the negotiation request with accepted parameters."
            "Master will use accepted parameters from response from the next transaction."

            uint8
            doc "Version of EMPSPI protocol, current version 0x1"
            default=0x1;

            doc         "Use Periodic report"
            enum {
                {0x01, "Used"},
                {0x00, "Unused"}
            }
            present_if ("CommandType" = 0x24);

        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;

        CC_PARAM "Data"
            uint8[9]
            doc  "Payload is either BT settings: <br>Num Completed packets Threshold <br>RSSI High Limit <br>high limit for Power control requests<br>RX RSSI low limit for A2DP   <br>LMP Timeout for RNR   <br>enable/disable status of HV1 reject feature  <br>WBS feature support status  <br>WBS feature enable status <br>CVSD Passthrough enable status <br>OR LE Settings:  <br>LEA buffer size <br>Max LE Connections  <br>Max LE Slave connections  <br>Max Available LE Connections  <br>Max Available LE Slave Connections";
    }

    COMMAND "LE_Meta_VSC" 0x0E9

            default = 0x1;

        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        # HCI_LE_ext_opcode = 0x01, "LE_Enable_CS_Features"
        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        PARAM "LE_Enable_CS_Features"
            uint32
            doc     "Enable/Disable Multiple LE Customer Specific Features through bit map."
            bitmap
            {
                {0x00000001, "LE Resolve Address - Whitelist"},

                {0x00020000, "LE BT and HID Coex Connection Block Info"},

        PARAM "Action"
            uint8
            doc     "Action for the operation"
                    "For LE_Audio_Select, options are: 0-disable, 1=enable, 2=read settings"
                    "For LE_Audio_Sync, options are: 0-disable, 1=enable, 2=read settings, 3=debug"
                    "For LE_Audio_Settings, options are: 0-write, 1=read"

                {0x0, "0"},
                {0x1, "1"},
                {0x2, "2"},
                {0x3, "3"}
            }
            present_if (("HCI_LE_ext_opcode" = 0x25) or ("HCI_LE_ext_opcode" = 0x26) or ("HCI_LE_ext_opcode" = 0x2E) or ("HCI_LE_ext_opcode" = 0x2F));

        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

        # HCI_LE_ext_opcode = 0x04, "LE Clear IRK List"
        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

                {0, "Add or Write"},
                {1, "Delete or Read"},
                {2, "Clear"},
                {3, "Read list entries"}
            }
            default = 0x0

            }
            default = 0x7
            present_if ("HCI_LE_ext_opcode" = 0x0E);

                {0x1C, "LE Get Zone Monitor Capacity"},

                {0x1E, "LE Reset Advanced Matching Rules"},
                {0x1F, "LE Remove Zone Entry"},

                {0x25, "LE_Audio_Select"},
                {0x26, "LE_Audio_Settings"},
                {0x27, "LE_Audio_GetCodecList"},
                {0x28, "LE_Audio_Create"},
                {0x29, "LE_Audio_Setting"},
                {0x2A, "LE_Audio_Config"},
                {0x2B, "LE_Audio_GetCodecList"},
                {0x2C, "LE_Audio_SilencePkt"},

                {0x34, "LE Add Device To White List With Parameters"},
                {0x35, "LE_Advanced_Matching_AddrList_Cfg"},
                {0x36, "LE Scan Cache"}
            };

        CC_PARAM "Action"
            uint8
            doc     "Action for the operation"
                    "For LE_Audio_Select/LE_Audio_Sync, options are: 0-disable, 1=enable, 2=read settings"
                    "For LE_Audio_Settings, options are: 0-write, 1=read"
            present_if ((("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x25) or ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x26) or ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x2A) or ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x2C) or ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x2E)) and ("Status" = 0));

                {0x00, "Public Address"},

            present_if ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x0D);

            bdaddr

                {0x01, "Enabled"}
            }
            present_if (("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x2F) and ("Action" != 0x03));

        CC_PARAM "Enable"
            uint8
            doc     "Enable/Debug"
            present_if (("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x2F) and ("Action" = 0x03));

        CC_PARAM "Codec"
            uint8
            doc     "Codec"
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "msbc"},
                {0x01, "msbc'"}
            }
            present_if ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x2F);


        # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
        # HCI_LE_ext_opcode = 0x2E, "LE_Sleep_Clock_Accuracy"
        # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
        CC_PARAM "Sleep_Clock_Accuracy"
            uint8
            doc "Sleep_Clock_Accuracy (SCA)"
            enum
            {
                {0x0, "251 ppm to 500 PPM"},
                {0x1, "151 ppm to 250 PPM"},
                {0x2, "101 ppm to 150 PPM"},
                {0x3, "76 ppm to 100 PPM"},
                {0x4, "51 ppm to 75 PPM"},
                {0x5, "31 ppm to 50 PPM"},
                {0x6, "21 ppm to 30 PPM"},
                {0x7, "0 ppm to 20 PPM"}
            }
            present_if ( ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x2E) and ("Status" = 0) );

        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        # Command Complete Event parameters for Event_LE_ext_Opcode 0x30
        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        CC_PARAM "LE_Conn_RSSI_History_Available_Memory"
            uint16
            doc "Return available memory"
            present_if ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x30);

        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        # Command Complete Event parameters for Event_LE_ext_Opcode 0xE, 0x31
        # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
        CC_PARAM "White_List_Attribute"
            uint8
            doc     "attribute"
            bitmap
            {
                {0x00, "Device Not In Any White List or White List is Empty"},
                {0x01, "SCAN White List"},
                {0x02, "INIT White List"},
                {0x04, "ADV White List"}
            }
            present_if ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x31);

        CC_PARAM "Number_Of_Empty_Entries_In_White_List"
            uint8
            doc     "Number Of Empty Entries left in White List"
            present_if ( "Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x31);

        # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
        # Command Complete Event parameters for Event_LE_ext_Opcode 0x34, "LE Add Device To White List With Parameters"
        # --------------------------------------------------------------------------

            uint8
            doc "Return number of available entries in the list of Device in White List with Connection Parameters"
            present_if ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x34);

        # --------------------------------------------------------------------------

        # --------------------------------------------------------------------------

            uint8

            present_if ("Event_LE_ext_Opcode" = 0x34);

    }

    COMMAND "Write_VS_ID" 0xFC
    {
        devs "*"=0
        doc "Writes an item to the configuration data volatile section"
        PARAM "ID"
            uint8

            min = 0x01
            max = 0x7B

            uint8

                    " which can be written is 250 bytes."
            binary_message_only
            encode_value = ByteArrayValidLength("VS_Data");
        PARAM "VS_Data"
            uint8[250]
            doc "The data to be written.  Only Length bytes of data are valid,"
                    "though the VS_Data parameter is fixed at a length of 250 bytes."
                "(The maximum VS item length is actually 255 bytes, but BlueTool"
                "requires all parameters to fit in 255 bytes.)"
            valid_length = "Length"
            min = 1                                 # Minimum array length in an array context
            max = 250;                              # Minimum array length in an array context
        CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;

            uint8

  COMMAND "Read_FW_Info" 0x0FE
       {
              devs  "*"=1
        doc "Generic command to get FW info. "
        PARAM "Info_type"
               uint8
               doc  "Info Type"
                enum
                { {0x01, "PC Workarounds"},
                   {0x02, "Local BPCS Features"},
                   {0x03, "Read De-sense Mode"},
                   {0x04, "Read UHE Mode"},
                   {0x05, "Read RSSI Offset Range"},
                   {0x80, "Read Sleep Xtal Gain"}};
            CC_PARAM "Status"
            errorcode8;
        CC_PARAM "Info_type"
            uint8
            doc "Info Type";
        CC_PARAM "Info_len"
            uint8
            doc "Info Length";
        CC_PARAM "PC_WA_bitmap"
            uint16
            doc "PC Workarounds bitmap"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x01);
        CC_PARAM "PC_WA_avoid_5_slot_packets"
            uint8
            doc "PC Workarounds Avoid 5 Slots Packets"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x01);
        CC_PARAM "PC_WA_delay_entering_sleep"
            uint8
            doc "PC Workarounds Delay Entering Sleep"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x01);
        CC_PARAM "PC_WA_USB_resume_duration"
            uint8
            doc "PC Workarounds USB Resume Duration"
            present_if ( ("Info_type" = 0x01) and ("Info_len" > 0x04) );
        CC_PARAM "PC_WA_Reserved"
            uint8
            doc "PC Workarounds Reserved"
            present_if ( ("Info_type" = 0x01) and ("Info_len" > 0x04) );
        CC_PARAM "PC_WA_Reserved"
            uint16
            doc "PC Workarounds Reserved"
            present_if ( ("Info_type" = 0x01) and ("Info_len" > 0x04) );
        CC_PARAM "PC_WA_Extended_BitMap"
            uint32
            doc "PC Workarounds Extended"
            present_if ( ("Info_type" = 0x01) and ("Info_len" > 0x04) );
        CC_PARAM "Local_BPCS_Features"
            uint32
            doc "Local BPCS Features"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x02);
        CC_PARAM "De-sense Mode"
            uint8
            doc "De-sense Mode"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x03)
            enum
            { {0x01, "Allow sECI to control de-sense"},
               {0x02, "Force BT to never be de-sensed"},
               {0x03, "Force BT to always be de-sensed"}
            };
        CC_PARAM "UHE Mode"
            uint8
            doc "UHE Mode"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x04)
            enum
            { {0x00, "UHE ON"},
               {0x01, "UHE Disabling"},
               {0x02, "UHE OFF"}
            };
        CC_PARAM "RSSI Offset Lower Limit"
            int8
            doc "RSSI Offset Lower Limit"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x05);
        CC_PARAM "RSSI Offset Upper Limit"
            int8
            doc "RSSI Offset Upper Limit"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x05);
        CC_PARAM "Sleep Xtal Gain"
            uint8
            doc "Sleep Xtal Gain"
            present_if ("Info_type" = 0x80)
            enum
            { {0x00, "Nominal gain"},
               {0x01, "Max Gain"}
            };

    }

COMMAND "Factory_Cal_Set_Tx_Power" 0x115
        {
       devs "*"=1, "BCM43342A0"=1
        doc "Turns on the TX according to the mode, frequency, and power specified by the parameters. The TX is modulated with a PRBS at a default TX trim setting"

    PARAM "Mode"
            uint8
            doc         "mode"
            enum {
                {0x00, "BDR"},
                {0x01, "EDR"},
        {0x02, "LE"},
        {0x10, "BDR_ePA"},
        {0x11, "EDR_ePA"},
        {0x12, "LE_ePA"},
        {0x20, "CountryCode0"},
        {0x21, "CountryCode1"},
        {0x30, "Tx_FrontEnd_config"},
        {0x40, "ultraFast_cal"},
        {0x41, "ultraFast_val"},
        {0x42, "ultraFast_config"},
        {0x43, "ultraFast_end"}
        }
        default = 0;
      PARAM "Channel"
            uint8
         doc  "BT channel number 0-78.Suggested channels are (8, 24, 40, 56, 72)   E.g. equivalent frequencies:[2410,2426,2442,2458,2474]"
            present_if (("Mode" < 0x13) or ("Mode" = 0x40) or ("Mode" = 0x41))      # jerome --- 02/19/2015
                #present_if ("Mode" < 0x13)
            min = 0
            max = 78
        default = 8;
    PARAM "Transmit Power"
            uint8
            doc         "TX output power level in dBm/.25.  range is -80 to 127 (-20 dBm to +20 dBm)"
        present_if ("Mode" < 0x13)
            min =0
            max = 127
            default = 0;

    PARAM "Pad Val"
            uint8
            doc         "For debugging, padVal can be forced using the padVal parameter.set to 0 as default"
        present_if ("Mode" < 0x13)
            min = 0
            max = 255
            default = 0;
    PARAM "ePA"
            uint8
            doc         "Select max power index for ePA/iPA"
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x30)
            min = 0
            max = 1
            default = 0;
    PARAM "Antenna"
            uint8
            doc         "Select Antenna"
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x30)
            min = 0
            max = 1
            default = 0;
    PARAM "Country Code"
            uint8
            doc         "Select Country Code"
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x30)
            min = 0
            max = 15
            default = 0;
    PARAM "Power Cap"
            uint8
            doc         "Select Power CAP"
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x30)
            min = 0
            max = 1
            default = 0;

# jerome --- 02/19/2015
    PARAM "Tx_mode"
            uint8
            doc "Tx_mode"
            default = 0
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x41)
            enum {
                {0x00, "BDR"},
                {0x01, "EDR"},
                {0x02, "LE"}
            }
        default = 0x00;
    PARAM "HP path enable"
            bool8
            doc         "Enable HP path i.e. select high power path"
            default = 1
            present_if ("Mode" = 0x41);
    PARAM "PAGC (pwrRamAd)"
            uint8
            doc         "Select PAGC pwrRam address"
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x41)
            min = 0
            max = 45
            default = 5;
    PARAM "PadVal"
            uint8
            doc         "Select PadVal value"
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x41)
            min = 0
            max = 255
            default = 0x32;

    PARAM "BDR_LE BB_Packet_Type"
            uint8
            doc "Sets the packet type."
                "Fixed pattern."
        present_if (("Mode" = 0x42) or (("Mode" = 0x41) and ("Tx_mode" != 0x01)))
            enum
            {
                {0x00, "LE_dummy"},
                {0x04, "DH1"},
                {0x0B, "DH3"},
                {0x0F, "DH5"}
            }
            default = 0x04;
    PARAM "BDR_LE BB_Packet_Length"
            uint16
            doc         "Select BB_Packet_Length for BDR_LE"
        present_if (("Mode" = 0x42) or (("Mode" = 0x41) and ("Tx_mode" != 0x01)))
            min = 0
            max = 0xffff
            default = 37;
    PARAM "EDR BB_Packet_Type"
            uint8
            doc "Sets the packet type."
                "Fixed pattern."
        present_if (("Mode" = 0x42) or (("Mode" = 0x41) and ("Tx_mode" = 0x01)))
            enum
            {
                {0x04, "2-DH1"},
                {0x08, "3-DH1"},
                {0x0A, "2-DH3"},
                {0x0B, "3-DH3"},
                {0x0E, "2-DH5"},
                {0x0F, "3-DH5"}
            }
            default = 0x08;
    PARAM "EDR BB_Packet_Length"
            uint16
            doc         "Select BB_Packet_Length for EDR"
        present_if (("Mode" = 0x42) or (("Mode" = 0x41) and ("Tx_mode" = 0x01)))
            min = 0
            max = 0xffff
            default = 50;

    PARAM "Repeat sequence"

            present_if ("Mode" = 0x42);
    PARAM "EDR bypass PAGC (pwrRamAd) max"
            uint8
            doc         "Select max bypass PAGC pwrRam address for EDR"
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x42)
            min = 0
            max = 45
            default = 0;
    PARAM "EDR bypass PAGC (pwrRamAd) min"
            uint8
            doc         "Select min bypass PAGC pwrRam address for EDR"
        present_if ("Mode" = 0x42)
            min = 0
            max = 45
            default = 6;
        PARAM "Modulation_Type"
            uint8
            doc         "Sets the data pattern that is continuously transmitted"
            present_if ("Mode" = 0x41)
            enum
            {
                {0x01, "0x00  8-bit Pattern"},
                {0x02, "0xFF  8-bit Pattern"},
                {0x03, "0xAA  8-bit Pattern"},
                {0x09, "0xF0  8-bit Pattern"},
                {0x04, "PRBS9 Pattern"}
            }
            default = 0x04;
# end of jerome --- 02/19/2015

COMMAND "Factory_Cal_Trim_Tx_Power" 0x116
        {
       devs "*"=1, "BCM43342A0"=1
      doc "Trims the output power by the specified dB value. This command is valid only if Factory_Cal_Set_Tx_Power is used earlier to setup and turn on the Tx<BR> Error is represented as dB/.25"

    PARAM "Error"
            int8
            doc         "Error is a signed value to instruct the chip to increase or decrease the power by the desired amount. Range is -16 to 16 (-4 dB to +4 dB)"
            min =-16
            max = 16
            default = 0;
    CC_PARAM "Status"
    errorcode8
    doc "Command complete status";
    CC_PARAM "Pad Value"
        uint8
        doc "New PAD value used for this output power setting";
        }
